title: How to read Korean from 0 - All the Basics you need | Korean Alphabet - Hangul
description:
#koreanalphabet  #learnkorean #hangul 
📚 Learn to Read the Korean Alphabet – Hangul for Absolute Beginners! 🇰🇷
In this first video of the Korean Alphabet Series, we’ll break down the basics of Hangul,
the Korean writing system — starting completely from zero!

You'll learn:
- What Hangul is and how it works
- How Korean syllable blocks are built
- The 10 basic vowels
- The 14 basic consonants
- How to read your first Korean word: 바나나 (banana)!

This class is designed for complete beginners,
so don’t worry about memorizing — just relax, follow along, and enjoy the sounds!

✨ New videos every week – We’ll practice writing, reading, and speaking Korean together step by step.

Still not sure if you’re learning Korean the right way?
Let me help you learn naturally — and with confidence.

title: Learn Korean Like a Baby | A0 #1 Basic Expressions (Comprehensible Input)
description:
📄 Today’s Key Expression and full script: [https://deliciouskorean.com/blog/learn-korean-like-a-baby-a0-1-basic-expressions?utm_source=yt250510_a0_1]

🗣️ Practice speaking Korean with a verified native tutor: [https://deliciouskorean.com?utm_source=yt250510_a0_1]

🍼 Want to learn Korean like a baby?
Welcome to the very first episode of our Learn Korean Like a Baby - A0 series!
Even if you don’t know any Hangul yet, this video is made just for you.
In today’s lesson, you'll hear 8 essential Korean expressions used in real context, all through a method called Comprehensible Input.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
💡 How to Get the Most Out of This Video:
✅ First, just watch and listen. Try to guess the meaning from the context.
✅ Second, watch again and speak out loud copying the sounds.
✅ Then, review the full script and practice the sentences at your own pace.
✅ Use the example phrases to make your own sentences!

📺 Haven’t seen the Intro Video yet?
👉 Watch it first to understand how this whole series works: [https://youtu.be/RsZjfHmPaLo]

🎬 Want to Keep Going?
▶️ Watch the full Learn Korean Like a Baby - A0 playlist here: [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=RsZjfHmPaLo&list=PLX4sCi4jjuqrUjr2QojxYpxlD-KIhwVMO&pp=gAQB]

title: 📺✨ Learn Korean Phrases with K-Drama: 헐! [heol] (slang)
description:
Today’s phrase is "헐", a popular reaction word in Korean dramas used when someone is shocked, surprised, or at a loss for words! It’s a super casual expression perfect for those moments when you’re just... speechless. 🤯

In English, "헐" can be translated as:

- OMG!
- No way!
- What?!
- Seriously?

Enjoy learning this fun expression with real K-drama moments! 🐰🤗

📚 Want to dive deeper into Korean grammar? Check out ‘Master Korean Grammar for Beginners’ (Levels 1, 2, and 3), an easy-to-follow series perfect for mastering the basics of Korean grammar.

title: Learn Hangul in 90 Minutes - Start to Finish [Complete Series]
description:
Learn Hangul quickly in only 90 minutes. Hangul is the Korean alphabet, and this video will cover everything you need to know from its history, to how to read and write it, and sound change rules for proper pronunciation. Get something to write with and start learning Korean the right way - from Hangul.

Want to start learning Korean? To take the next step, check out my book, "Korean Made Simple" on Amazon: http://amzn.to/2bDBi6h (affiliate)

This video is a compilation of the videos from this playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLbFrQnW0BNMVrHEJCOYWbLnXFzj4bl5z0

Why did I put all of these videos together? For people who just want to watch 1 video and be done with the whole thing. If you have more time, check out the videos individually in the playlist.

title: 📺Learn Korean Phrases with K-Drama: "When are you coming?"
description:
Today's expression is "언제 와?", which means "When are you coming?" in English. This is a simple and commonly used question in daily conversations when you're asking someone about their arrival time. 

📝 Breakdown  
언제 [eon·je]: when  
와 [wa]: come (casual form of 오다)

Polite form: 언제 와요?


I hope this video is interesting and helpful to you! 🐰🤗

title: 50 Essential Korean Words Every Beginner Must Know - Learn Korean with your favorite Kdramas!
description:
In this video you can learn 50 very common and useful Korean words with examples from many different Korean dramas. Note that most of these words and expressions are informal and would normally only be used with people you are familiar with like friends or family, but they are nevertheless used very often and you will hear them frequently in Korean.
Also be aware that Korean and English are very different languages and it is not always easy to directly translate the meaning or pronunciation of a Korean word. 

For example, in Korean the "L" is often pronounced closer to an English rolling "R".
Also, when a Korean word starts with an "M" as in for instance [moleugesseo], the "M" is denasalized in Korean making it sound closer to an English "B". It's comparable to saying "moleugesseo" while pinching your nose or having a cold!
Similarly, the "N" at the beginning of Korean words often sounds closer to an English "D", as in for instance [naeil].


Check out my channel for lots more! :)

title: 5 Best Podcasts for Korean Learners
description:
Learning Korean with podcasts can be a fun and effective way to improve your language skills. Not only do they help improve listening skills but also provide access to native speakers and a range of accents and dialects.

So, here are 5 best podcasts to listen to.

5 . Cozy Storytime in Korean
Length: 4 - 7 minutes
Level: Beginner and up 
Best for improving listening skills. Teaches about Korean culture and many more everyday things in Korean through bedtime stories.

4. KingKa: A K Drama Podcast
Length: 30 - 60 minutes
Level: Beginner and up
Uses K-drama as a platform to discuss elements of culture and language.

3. Intermediate Korean
Length: 5 - 15 minutes
Level: Intermediate / Advanced
Uses natural language and stories to teach listeners important linguistic concepts and grammar as well as elements of Korean culture and history.

2. Breathe in Korean
Length: 10 - 20 minutes
Level: Intermediate / Advanced
It has inspiring quotations from various literary works in Korean and English. It's both informative and motivating, as well as relaxing. For those who need more exposure and listening practice.
  

1. Talk to Me in Korean
Length:  3 - 20 minutes
Level: All Levels
Lesson content is interesting and engaging, with episodes varying in length and difficulty. title: Korean is So Easy 😮 #korean
description:
Korean is really easy with this Magic word 네!😂
You can deliver so many things with this one word.
    1. Yes - 네
    2. I understand!- 아~네!
    3. I don’t understand - 네?
    4. Got it- 넵!
    5. Bye(on phone) - 네네네네 네~
    
You might have seen 네 so many times in K-drama or interview! Korean love to use this expression! 🥰

title: 📺Learn Korean Phrases with K-Drama: ”I’ll do it“ #kdrama #learningkorean #realkorean #korean
description:
Today’s expression is ”내가 할게,“ meaning ”I’ll do it“ or ”I’ll take care of it.“ This phrase is commonly used when volunteering to do something or taking responsibility for a task. You can use it in casual situations among friends or close peers.

📝 Breakdown
내가 [nae·ga]: I (subject, casual)
하다 [ha·da]: to do
할게 [hal·ge]: a form meaning ”I will do“

In polite language, you can say ”제가 할게요“ [je·ga hal·kke·yo], where ”제가“ is a more respectful form of ”I,“ and ”-요“ adds a polite ending.

Use this phrase to show helpfulness or readiness to act! 😊

📚 Want to dive deeper into Korean grammar? Check out ‘Master Korean Grammar for Beginners’ (Levels 1, 2, and 3), an easy-to-follow series perfect for mastering the basics of Korean grammar. Available as an eBook on our website and in paperback on Amazon. Click the link in my bio/profile! 👉 @tomikorean

title: Let’s Practice Speaking Korean Together! 🔥 3-Step Training: Listen, Speak & Break It Down
description:
📌 Useful Tips (not mentioned in the video)

1. Catching Rabbits? 🐇
→ This isn’t something I made up, and it's a real Korean expression! 😁 In Korean, instead of "catching birds" as in the English phrase "catch two birds with one stone," we often say "catching rabbits." 🐇 Of course, we do use the hanja proverb 일석이조 (一石二鳥) that means "one(일) stone(석), two(이) birds(조)," but in native Korean, 토끼 (rabbits) is more frequently used.

2. Speech Levels in the Conversation
→ Both the professor and student use 존댓말 (polite speech) in this conversation. But if you listen closely, you’ll notice that the student speaks more respectfully and uses honorific verbs like -(으)시다 and 드리다 often. In contrast, the professor never uses these forms. When creating this dialogue, I imagined about a 20-year age gap between them. In real life, it would sound unnatural for someone much older, like the professor, to use overly formal speech with a student. (This shows how, even within the same 존댓말, the use or omission of honorific verbs can make the speech sound simpler or more respectful.)

🔗 If you’d like to learn more about honorific verbs, you can watch this class: https://youtu.be/v7oFvmlrKD8?si=BBTNXLiw6gl7Tj1W

3. Is This Video Too Difficult for Beginners?
→ This content is ideal for intermediate learners, but even beginners can benefit! If you picked up just a few phrases or vocabulary, that’s a great start! 😊

📄⬇️ Conversation Script in PDF: https://explorekorean.net/extra-class-notes/?vid=37

자음 consonant 
14 basic consonant
ㄱ, ㄴ, ㄷ, ㄹ, ㅁ, ㅂ, ㅅ,
ㅇ, ㅈ, ㅊ, ㅋ, ㅌ, ㅍ, ㅎ
기역  ㄱ  g,  니은  ㄴ  n,
디귿  ㄷ  d,  리을 l
미음  ㅁ m, 비읍 ㅂ b
시옷  ㅅ  s,  이응  ㅇ  silent/ng,
지읒  ㅈ  j,   치읓  ㅊ ch, 
키읔 ㅋ k  티읕 ㅌ t,
피읖  ㅍ  p,  히읗  ㅎ h

모음 vowels,
ㅏa, ㅑya,  ㅓuh,  ㅕyuh,  ㅣi,
ㅗoh,  ㅛyoh,  ㅜooh,  ㅠyooh,  ㅡeu,

Vocabulary 
나비 butterfly,  도시 city,  하마 hippo,
나라 country,  소녀 a girl,  아기 baby, 
커피 coffee,  오이 cucumber,  우리  us/we, 
다리 bridge or leg, 

Vocabulary 2 
아니 no,   아니요 no,  바구니 basket,
버스 bus,  우유  milk,  이야기 story,
모자 hat/cap,  파스타 pasta,  라자냐 lasagna, 

Vowels 2
ㅐae,  ㅒyae,  ㅔae,  ㅖyae,
ㅚ wae,  ㅘ wa,  ㅙ wae,
ㅟ wi,  ㅝ wuh,  ㅞ wae,
ㅢ ui, 

Vocabulary 3,
애기 talk story,  새 bird,  네 yes (formal),
예쁘다, 왜,  과자 snack,
쉐이크 shake, 웨이터 waiter, 의자 chair,
위 up, 뒤 behind, 

쌍자음 double consonant, 
ㄲ Spanish g,  ㄸ Spanish d,  ㅃ p as in apple,
ㅆ s as i sun,  ㅉ zh(dj) as in 真(zhen) 

받침 Batchim (final consonant)
ㄱ 각 낙 막 박,  ㄴ 간 잔 칸 판,
ㄷ 닫 맏 믿,       ㄹ 갈 날 달 말,
ㅁ 감 남 담,       ㅂ 밥 삽 깁 흡,
ㅅ 깃 놋 닷 옷,  ㅇ 방탄소년단 BTS ,
ㅈ 갖 겆 짖,       ㅊ 읓 젗 닻 돛, 
ㅋ 해질녘 부엌, ㅍ same as ㅂ,
ㅎ 좋아  놓다,

쌍자음 
ㄲ 낚 삮  same as 닉 삭
ㅆ 했 있  same as 햇 잇

title: Korean Grammar for Absolute Beginners | Word Order & Core Particles (은/는, 이/가, 을/를)
description:
00:00 Ice Break (Skip)
02:10 Korean Word Order Practice 
20:10 Topic Particle 은/는
31:20 Subject Particle 이/가
49:58 Object Particle 을/를
01:14:40 Heads-up for next class

This class is for learners who are just starting Korean grammar! 📝

We’ll start from the very basics — like simple sentence structure and the core particles 은/는, 이/가 and 을/를, — and I’ll explain them in a way that’s easy to understand even if you’re just getting started. We’ll also practice making simple sentences together.

It’s recommended that you’re already somewhat familiar with Hangeul, the Korean alphabet; you don’t need to be 100% confident yet, but having a basic understanding of Korean consonants, vowels, and batchim will help. If you’ve never learned Hangeul at all, it might be a bit difficult to follow.

I'm excited about this class!🙌🏻✨

(📕The book I’m using on my iPad in this video is the companion workbook to my grammar book. It's titled "Korean Grammar Canvas for Beginners 1: Workbook," and it’s expected to be published this November. I’ll keep you guys updated when it’s released!)

title: Korean Grammar & Speaking Practice: -ㄹ/을 텐데, 테니까 (What's this "테"?)
description:
-ㄹ/을 텐데 and -ㄹ/을 테니까 are among the most frequently asked questions I get from intermediate students. Honestly, organizing the usage of -ㄹ/을 테 was a bit of a headache because it wasn't easy to come up with a definitive grammatical explanation for this. That’s why, instead of focusing on what’s absolutely correct from a grammatical perspective, I prioritized how these expressions are actually used in real-life conversations, like what’s more commonly heard in everyday speech. This video reflects the most common and practical usage of these expressions in modern spoken Korean :) So please keep this in mind as you learn! 

00:00 Intro
00:24 -ㄹ/을 테 vs. -ㄹ/을 거
02:08 -ㄹ/을 텐데
04:35 Quiz & Speaking Practice
06:32 -ㄹ/을 테니까
09:32 Speaking Practice
10:32 Recap
10:45 Outro

title: All About 받침 (Batchim): Korean Final Consonants | ㄱ to ㅎ Reading Practice (Part#1)
description:
Here is the lesson on Consonant Assimilation
https://youtu.be/x3kwxK1b94I

For the entire Hangeul Playlist, check here : 
https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLECz2rpRD3Z0EeOU0z3aoafwgjHyth7MN&si=xLbVAqpoLstanrHR

📚 All-You-Need Korean for Absolute Beginners
Paperback: https://amzn.to/3zzB1Ot
eBook (full) : https://a.co/d/9N8ofjZ
eBook (Hangeul Chapter Only): https://a.co/d/g5kQu3q

To preview the book's content, feel free to watch this video:
https://youtu.be/nShFk5-HIZM?si=qJhufQtaX4hR0GNP
-------------------------------------------------------------------
This lesson would be difficult to follow if you haven't taken the basic 한글 (Hangul, Korean alphabets) lesson yet. In that case, you can start your first hangul lesson here:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=85qJXvyFrIc

받침 (batchim, aka the final consonant) is part of hangul that confuses learners the most. So I made separate lessons (part1, part2, part 3 - the other two will be up as well) to explain more in-depth about 받침. It's a long lesson because I've included a lot of read-it-together practice. If you find it too long, you can always speed up the video or skip to only the parts you need help with.

I'll see you in the next part 2 video (the link is at the top). We'll learn more about 자음동화 (consonant assimilation) a little of which we've also covered in this vid at ㅎ받침 section and at the very last bit.

title: All About 받침 Part 2 - Consonant Assimilation | Sound Like a Native Speaker
description:
You can check the updated video lesson on Consonant Assimilation (with more example vocabs and sentences) here: https://youtu.be/x3kwxK1b94I

Part 3 Lesson is up in the link below!
Reading Words with ㄳ, ㄵ, ㄶ, ㄼ, ㄾ, ㅀ, ㅄ, ㄺ, ㄻ as Final Consonants:
https://youtu.be/Qo-Tp409sKE
••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••­­­•••••••••••
Video Description

받침 Part 2 - Here we learn how to read words and sentences where consonant assimilation (자음동화) happens! I know it gets trickier to understand in some parts, but as I always say, with more encounter and practice with various words and sentences, you'll get used to it and you'll also get to a level where you automatically make the correct pronunciation without even thinking.  Of course, that doesn't happen overnight, so I suggest you keep studying words and practice how to read them. Making sound while memorizing will help the words stick in your head a lot longer than otherwise as well :)


#Korean #FinalConsonant #Batchim

title: All Basics about "은, 는 & 이, 가" (Subject Particles in Korean)

title: 은/는 & 이/가 (Subject Marking Particles) Explained
description:
When to use 은/는 and 이/가 is one of what Korean learners find the most confusing to understand. Native Korean speakers can easily tell the difference, because we’ve grown up in this language our entire life, but we find it very difficult to explain this to non-natives. There are numerous contexts when using a language and it would be impossible to verbalize all of them, so I doubt there can be one single, clear answer to 은/는/이/가 confusion. But I do hope that the explanation and example sentences I have for you in this lesson help you inch closer to getting it. 화이팅, everyone! 💪

(If you want to skip right on to 은/는 & 이/가 comparison, you can start from 08:27 If you want to know what a particle and a subject marker is in Korean language, you're welcome to stay with me all the way from the beginning 😊) 

#은는이가 #KoreanParticles

title: Korean Numbers! (Sino & Native)
description:
Learn how to say numbers in Korean with this video, in both Sino and Native Korean! :)

e.g. 
[sino]
1 일 il 
2 이 ee 
3 삼  sam 
4 사 sa 
5 오 o 
6 육 yook 
7 칠 chil 
8 팔 pal 
9 구 goo

[native(pure)]
1 하나 hana
2 둘 dool
3 셋 set 
4 넷 net
5 다섯 dasut 
6 여섯 yuhsut 
7 일곱 ilgob 
8 여덟 yuhdul 
9 아홉 ahob 
10 열 yeol

#KoreanNumber #한국어숫자

title: How Many Slang Words Do We Know? (feat. Billy from Go! Billy Korean)
description:
Billy's Channel: https://www.youtube.com/user/GoBillyKorean
Go! Billy Korean Website: https://gobillykorean.com/
••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••­­­•••••••••••
Billy and I played a little game where we test each other on how many slang words in our second language we know, among the 10 words each of us brought. 

Who do you think would win the game?! Haha. 

There’s also a 벌칙 at the end, which is basically a punishment for the loser. We both did our best at this game to avoid 벌칙 😆 Hope you enjoy the vid and also get to know some expressions you didn’t know before, both in English and Korean!

*Just in case you're wondering, we used Zoom for recording. What's great about it is that it automatically alternates two videos according to who's speaking at the moment, so it makes it much more convenient to edit :)

title: 10 Real Korean Conversations at Restaurants & Cafes | For Korean Beginners (TOPIK 1)
description:
☕🇰🇷 **Learn Real Korean Conversations at Cafes & Restaurants!**

Are you learning Korean but still nervous about ordering coffee or food in Korea?
Don’t worry — this video’s got you covered! 😎

In this lesson, you’ll learn **real-life Korean phrases and conversations** that locals actually use at **cafés and restaurants**.
No boring textbook lines — just natural Korean you can use right away when visiting Korea or talking with Korean friends.

🎯 Perfect for **Korean beginners** who want to:
✅ Learn how to order food and drinks in Korean
✅ Sound more natural and confident when speaking
✅ Understand what Koreans really say (not the stiff textbook version!)

By the end of this video, you’ll know exactly how to order your latte, ask for the menu, and chat with servers like a true local. ☕🍜

So hit play, practice along, and get ready to **speak real Korean with confidence!** 💬✨

#LearnKorean #KoreanForBeginners #KoreanConversation #CafeKorean #RestaurantKorean #SpeakLikeAKorean

title: Korean Conversation with John | 이렇게 귀하신 분이 ③
description:
오늘은 저의 유튜버 친구 존(John)과 한국어 학습에 대한 이야기를 나누었어요.
존은 '존쌤의 언어습득법'이라는 유튜브 채널을 운영하며 외국어를 원어민처럼 하고 싶어 하는 사람들에게 팁을 주는 영상을 만들고 있어요. 저는 작년에 우연히 존의 유튜브 영상을 발견하게 되었어요. 그 영상에서 저는 제가 원했던 언어 공부 방법을 찾게 되었고, 정말 획기적이라고 생각했어요. 존과는 제가 남겨놓았던 댓글을 계기로 빠르게 친해지게 되었어요.

이번 팟캐스트에서 존은 정말 유익한 정보를 많이 공유해 주었고, 한국어를 공부하는 여러분에게도 새로운 시각을 제공해 줄 수 있을 것 같아요.
'한국어 어떻게 공부하면 좋을까? 한국어를 한국인처럼 잘 하고 싶어! 한국어 공부를 하는데 실력이 잘 늘지 않아...🤔' 특히 이런 생각을 하고 있는 분들이라면, 분명 많은 도움이 될 거라고 생각합니다.
오늘도 한국어 공부 화이팅입니다 ❤️‍🔥

0:00 인트로
0:47 게스트 소개
1:30 존이 공부하는 언어들
3:20 외국어/한국어를 배우게 된 이유
7:17 한국어를 공부했던 방법
14:45 시각화의 중요성
19:49 너무 오래걸리는 거 아니야? - ⭐️한국인처럼 한국어를 하는 법⭐️
35:05 처음 한국어 배웠을 때로 돌아간다면? - 시청 듣기
42:50 재미없어 보여!
46:50 영어 원어민이 배우기에 한국어는 어렵다? - 한국어 문법

title: Korean: Listening Practice (Level 1, Dialogues 1 - 12)
description:
If you want to practice your Korean Listening skills, watch this video.

While learning a language, it is very important to practice your listening skills regularly. Otherwise, you will be like a "walking textbook" who can neither speak nor understand the speech you hear, even though you know all the grammar and words. 
If you don't practice listening skills you will understand the language only on paper.

In this video you will listen to 12 dialogues. All the grammar that is used in these dialogues, I explained in the previous lessons, as well as 90% of the words used in the dialogues you should also know.
You can check out Listening playlist for more: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLJ8SeVAoE0L4cGkmhuRHIpWGOgY3SCwYL


You will listen to each dialogue 4 times.
1. Just audio without text
Try not to panic, concentrate and just listen and understand what is being said.

2. Audio + text
Listen to audio and look at text in Korean.
Look at the text and check if you understood the speech correctly when you listened to it for the first time.
Also, if you are in doubt whether you have read or heard the audio correctly, you can check it in the Latin text on the right side.
(I am not using the standard romanization rules because instead of making it easier to learn and understand Korean, they are rather confusing.)

3. Audio + text in Korean + text in English
Here you can check if you understood correctly what it is about.

4. Audio without text
Listen to the text again, don't look anywhere, concentrate. The audio speed is slightly faster here.

It would be more efficient to review all the grammar and vocabulary from the previous lessons before listening to the video.

If you liked this video, tell me in the comments, and I will try to make more such videos.
Also, if there is something I can improve, I would be glad if you share your thoughts.

Good luck! :)

title: Talking about Your Day #1 | 10 Short Korean Conversations | Beginner Korean | A1-A2-B1
description:
Boost your Korean listening skills with 10 realistic daily life conversations! ✨ 

In this video, we cover common scenarios like meeting friends at a cafe, shopping, and chatting at the library. Each conversation is played at two speeds: Slow (for clear pronunciation) and Normal (for natural flow). Perfect for Beginner to Intermediate learners (A1-A2-B1).

📝 Personal Note: 
Drafting these 10 conversations was quite a nostalgic process for me! I tried to recall the specific struggles I had when I first started speaking Korean—like what to say when running into a friend unexpectedly or how to make small talk about a tiring day. I focused on including the natural phrases I wish I had known back then. I hope you find them helpful and relatable!

⚠️ Disclaimer:
The content presented in this video is fictional and created solely for educational purposes. Please note that AI technology was used to assist in the production of this video to ensure high-quality learning material.

description:
❤️ Stop practicing alone! Speak Korean in real time with a native Korean coach 🙋🏻‍♀️ [https://deliciouskorean.com/?utm_source=yt250819_howlonglearned]

🇰🇷 Meeting a Korean for the first time is exciting… but also a little stressful. What do you say? How do you respond when they ask questions?

In this video, we’ll cover the *must-know* phrases every Korean asks:
💬 “How long have you studied Korean?”
💬 “Why are you learning Korean?”

You’ll not only learn them, but practice with me through shadowing — listen, repeat, and speak out loud with confidence.

✨ Perfect for beginners or anyone who wants to sound natural when meeting Koreans in real life.

✅ Essential first-meeting phrases

title: Britishers Making Pronunciation🤡😑 #englishgrammar #funny #english #studentlife #british
Why Does Everybody Sing Like THIS now? 🎤🤫 #billieeilish #music
description:
Why does everybody sing like this now? 🎤💨
From Billie Eilish’s breathy vocals in What Was I Made For? to Gracie Abrams’ soft whispers in I Miss You, I’m Sorry, today’s artists are intentionally changing the way they sing. But why? We’re breaking down 6 songs to find out how whisper-singing took over pop music. From Ariana Grande’s airy, breathy vocals in We Can’t Be Friends compared to her fuller sound in Break Up With Your Girlfriend, to Sabrina Carpenter’s drastic shift from Looking At Me to Espresso, artists are leaning into intimate, emotional sounds by singing super close to the mic. But… does it actually sound better? Let’s find out. 

Thank you Earthfueled for the animations!!

#rickreverie #BillieEilish #GracieAbrams #ArianaGrande #SabrinaCarpenter #PopMusic #WhisperSinging #VocalTrends #MusicTrivia #ViralSongs #HitMusic

Facebook co-founder and CEO Mark Zuckerberg speaks fluent Mandarin at a question and answer session in Beijing.

He lists three reasons why he picked up the language, one of it being the challenge itself.

title: Putin Laughs at Suggestion to Export Pork to Muslim Majority Indonesia
description:
Vladimir Putin couldn’t hold his laughter in when Minister of Agriculture Alexander Tkachov suggested exporting pork to Muslim-majority Indonesia.

During the October 13 meeting on the development of agriculture, Tkachov proposed increasing pork production for exports to China and Indonesia. Putin responded, “Indonesia is a Muslim country. They do not eat pork there.”

Tkachov defended his proposal saying, “It does not matter. They will eat it.”

Through his laughter, Putin responded, “They won’t.”

In the Muslim religion, eating pork is prohibited.

Via President of Russia

We know how daunting it can feel to master a new language. That's why we created this channel - to provide a supportive, judgment-free space where you can build confidence, expand your vocabulary, and perfect your fluency.

Whether you're just starting out or consider yourself an advanced speaker, you'll find a wealth of resources tailored to your level and learning style. Our video lessons cover a wide range of topics, from conversational Italian and business communication, to grammar rules and pronunciation tips.

So whether you're preparing for an important presentation, traveling abroad, or simply want to be able to chat with Italian-speaking friends, consider this channel your personal language coach for improving both your Italian listening and speaking skills. Hit that subscribe button, engage with our community, and let's start your journey to Italian fluency together!

~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

Il nostro obiettivo è fornire contenuti divertenti, coinvolgenti e interattivi per aiutare gli studenti di tutti i livelli a migliorare le loro abilità in italiano.

Sappiamo quanto possa essere intimidante padroneggiare una nuova lingua. Per questo abbiamo creato questo canale - per offrire uno spazio di supporto e senza giudizi in cui potete costruire fiducia in voi stessi, ampliare il vostro vocabolario e perfezionare la vostra fluidità.

Che siate alle prime armi o vi consideriate parlanti avanzati, troverete una ricchezza di risorse adattate al vostro livello e al vostro stile di apprendimento. Le nostre lezioni video coprono una vasta gamma di argomenti, dall'italiano conversazionale alla comunicazione aziendale, dalle regole grammaticali ai consigli sulla pronuncia.

Che vi stiate preparando per una importante presentazione, stiate viaggiando all'estero o vogliate semplicemente poter chiacchierare con amici italofoni, considerate questo canale il vostro coach linguistico personale per migliorare sia la vostra comprensione dell'italiano che le vostre abilità di conversazione. Cliccate sul pulsante di iscrizione, interagite con la nostra community e iniziamo insieme il vostro percorso verso la fluidità in italiano!

title: Во что превратился Нью-Йорк: почему здесь страшно жить
description:
Нью-Йорк, США, Америка, эмиграция и жизнь экспатов. В этом фильме вы увидите реальный Нью-Йорк через жизни эмигрантов, переехавших в США в разные времена. Это честные истории о том, сколько стоит жизнь в Нью-Йорке, как устроена работа, медицина, полиция и бизнес в США, почему «американская мечта» больше не выглядит как в кино и кому вообще стоит переезжать в Америку, а кому лучше оставить Нью-Йорк красивой картинкой в фильмах.  

✅ Мой курс по интернет маркетингу + AI https://bit.ly/4u3poXl

🔥 Оставляйте заявку, сейчас действуют специальные условия  
📞 +380 507 41 16 93 Юлия (Telegram, Viber, WhatsApp)  
email: hello@para.school  

💻 Мое агентство по интернет-маркетингу: https://bit.ly/4uYvSH8
Звони или пиши: +380 950 82 16 52  
email: hello@para.agency  

Мой сервис для путешественников: https://bit.ly/48NwUNK  
iOS приложение: https://apps.apple.com/ua/app/tripmydream/id1523289196  

Леонид в 15 лет приехал в Нью-Йорк с мамой. Он расскажет, каким был шок после переезда, как он выживал в первые годы, как менялся Нью-Йорк

Леонид в Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/talk2lenny/

Виталий приехал по программе Work and Travel в США, а сейчас у него свой парк велорикш, который возит людей по городу и по Центральному парку. 

Виталий в Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/peterpentours/

Саша – фотограф с большим опытом. Он покажет, где живёт, чем примечателен его дом и почему стиральная машина в квартире – большая удача для Нью-Йорка. Поговорим о городских сумасшедших и поддержке от государства, о бесконечных стройках, зайдём в мишленовский ресторан и обсудим реальные цены в заведениях США.

Саша в Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/slyadnev/

Мы зайдём в кофейню, которую открыл Александр из Харькова. Он объяснит, почему выбрал для бизнеса именно Нью-Йорк, как сложно было найти помещение, где проще вести своё дело – в Украине или США. Александр расскажет о проблемных клиентах, попытках расплатиться фальшивыми купюрами и кражах, а также о реакции полиции в Америке.

Александр в Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/voievodinas/

Катя приехала из Киева совсем недавно. С ней обсудим бездомных и людей с ментальными проблемами, как вести себя с ними, чтобы не попасть в неприятности. Катя поделится своим разочарованием в Нью-Йорке и объяснит, почему этот город так и не стал её мечтой, хотя многие едут сюда за новой жизнью в США.

Катя в Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/hutpix/

По вопросам сотрудничества и если хотите пригласить меня к себе в ваш город и страну, пишите на почту: 909arasatma@gmail.com  

В этом выпуске о Нью-Йорке и жизни в США:

title: One-Stop Guide to Korean Particles - 은/는/이/가 (Subject & Topic Markers)
description:
If you're one of the millions of Korean learners who have struggled to understand exactly how the Korean particles 은, 는, 이 and 가 work, this will be a great one-stop guide for you! People who learn Korean as their mother tongue can distinguish their specific roles and various usages at a very young age, but for learners who study Korean as a foreign language, these topic/subject markers can be a bit tricky to understand. 

Here's the gist of this video: 
- 은 and 는 are "topic markers" and show you what the topic of the sentence is. 
- 이 and 가 are "subject markers" and show you the direct subject of the verb that is being used in the sentence.
- 은/는 also shows contrast and change of scene/narrative. 
- In compound sentences, such as "The movie I watched was fun.", you can see 은/는 and 이/가 being used together. 

But for a better understanding, we recommend that you watch the full video (more than once if necessary) and pay close attention to the detailed examples.

 ----------------------------------

Thanks for watching and studying Korean with our lessons!

title: Korean Stories For Language Learners (Review) [Traditional Folktales]
description:
More info about the book here: https://www.mezzoguild.com/review-korean-stories-for-language-learners/
Other Korean resources here: https://www.mezzoguild.com/korean-resources/

I recently received a copy of Korean Stories For Language Learners by Julie Damron and EunSun You (Tuttle Publishing).

The book covers 42 total short stories in Korean with English translations, comprehension exercises, vocab lists and full audio listening material.

Since the 'short story' niche of learning material seems to be trending at the moment, I thought I'd take time to check this one out. Rather than covering typical dialogue material, it focuses on traditional Korean folktales. The audio is a pleasure to listen to.

A few minor things to point out: absolute beginner material is inappropriate for this book (there's not much of it so it's not a major issue) and some exercises are borderline bizarre (but still funny).

Something a dedicated Korean student who loves Korean history and culture would definitely enjoy reading.

title: 📺Learn Korean Phrases with K-Drama: "sometimes"
description:
Today's expression is "가끔," which means "sometimes" in English. You’ll often hear this word when someone is talking about something they do occasionally or from time to time, but not regularly. It’s a great word to know for everyday conversations!

📝 Breakdown  
가끔 [ga-kkeum]: sometimes, occasionally  

It’s used to describe the frequency of an action:
- 가끔 영화 봐요. = I watch movies sometimes.  
- 가끔 혼자 있고 싶어요. = Sometimes I want to be alone.


I hope this video is interesting and helpful to you! 🐰🤗


📚 Ready to master Korean with ease? 

Check out our ‘Master Korean Grammar for Beginners’ and ‘Master Korean Words for Beginners’ series! Available as eBooks on our website and in paperback on Amazon. Click the link in my bio/profile! 👉 @tomikorean

title: 📺Learn Korean Phrases with K-Drama: "Come in"
description:
Today's expression is "들어와," which means "Come in" in English. You’ll often hear this phrase when someone is inviting another person into a room, house, or space. It’s a simple and friendly way to welcome someone. 

📝 Breakdown  
들다 [deu·reo·o·da]: to come in 
→ 들어와 = "Come in" (casual form of 들어오다)

Polite forms:  
- 들어와요 [deu·reo·wa·yo]  
- 들어오세요 [deu·reo·o·se·yo] (more formal / respectful)

I hope this video is interesting and helpful to you! 🐰🤗

title: 📺Learn Korean Phrases with K-Drama: "With whom?"
description:
누구랑? [nu·gu·rang]

Today's expression is "누구랑?", which means "With whom?" in English. This casual question is often used when you're curious or surprised about who someone is with. You’ll hear this a lot in dramas when someone is feeling jealous, suspicious, or just nosey!

📝 Breakdown
누구 [nu·gu]: who
-랑 [-rang]: with (particle "(이)랑")

"누구랑?" is a shortened and casual way of asking "Who are you with?" (누구랑 있어? / 누구랑 갔어?). The tone and context can totally change the vibe—sometimes playful, sometimes dramatic!

I hope this video is interesting and helpful to you! 🐰🤗

title: 11 Typical Korean Expressions - Learn Korean with Kdramas!
description:
In this video you will learn 11 very common Korean expressions or exclamations. Most of these are versatile words that can't be translated in a single way but can have multiple meanings depending on the situation or the way they are said. This makes them very useful, but also more difficult to understand for Korean learners, which is why I've added some example situations for every word to show you how they are used.

The words covered in this video are:

헐 [heol] meaning "Wow / OMG / No way!"
설마 [seolma] meaning "No way / Surely not / Don't tell me..."
어머 [eomeo] meaning "Oh! / Oh my / Oh my goodness"
혹시 [hoksi] meaning "Perhaps / By any chance"
역시 [yeoksi] meaning "As expected / As I thought / I knew it"
대박 [daebak] meaning "Wow / Amazing / Awesome"
하긴 [hagin] meaning "I guess / I suppose / That figures"
그냥 [geunyang] meaning "Just / Just because / It's just that / No reason"
인정 [injeong] meaning "(I) admit / agree / acknowledge"
거봐 [geobwa] meaning "See? (I told you)"
아이고 / 아이구 [aigo / aigu] meaning "Oh! / Oh dear / Oh no / Gosh"

Check out my channel for lots more!

title: 📺Learn Korean Phrases with K-Drama: "How much is it?"
description:
Today's expression is "얼마예요?", which means "How much is it?" in English. This is a super useful phrase to know for shopping, especially when you're traveling or buying something in Korea. You can use it to politely ask the price of an item—whether at a street market, cafe, or store.

📝 Breakdown  
얼마 [eol·ma]: how much  
예요 [ye·yo]: polite ending (equivalent to "is it")

Polite form: 얼마예요?  
Casual form: 얼마야?

💡 Tip: You can point to an item and say "이거 얼마예요?" to ask "How much is this?"

I hope this video is interesting and helpful to you!

title: 5 K-Dramas That’ll Actually Help You Learn Korean
Top 10 Super Easy Korean words - Learn Korean with your favorite Kdramas!
description:
In this video you can learn 10 very easy common words in Korean.
In Korean the level of politeness in your speech is important, depending on who you are talking to. These 10 words are mostly for informal situations, for example with friends, family, or people of similar age as you.

The 10 words covered in this video are:
Hey! - written in Korean as 야 and pronounced as [ya]
You - 너 pronounced as [neo]. Note that the N at the beginning of a Korean word is often denasalized, making it sound closer to an English D. It's similar to saying "neo" while having a cold or pinching your nose.
Excuse me - 저기 pronounced as [jeogi], it is used to get someone's attention
Sorry - 미안 pronounced as [mian]. Note that similar to the N, the M at the beginning of Korean words is often denasalized, making it sound closer to an English B. It's similar to saying "mian" while having a cold or pinching your nose.
Hi/Bye - 안녕 pronounced as [annyeong]. A common way to informally say Hi when meeting someone, but can also be said when leaving.
Thanks - 고마워 pronounced as [gomawo]
Fine/I like it - 좋아 pronounced as [joh-a], it is often used when you agree with someone's suggestion, but can also be used to say that you like something (or someone).
I know - 알아 pronounced as [ala], note that the L sounds closer to a rolling R, though it is not exactly an English L or R, but somewhere in between.
Okay/Sure - 그래 pronounced as [geulae]
Go/Bye - 가 pronounced as [ga], often used when saying goodbye to someone. It might sound strange to simply say "Go" to someone leaving, but in Korean this is quite normal and has no negative connotation, it can be compared to saying "Take care" in English.

Check out my channel for more! :)

title: 📺Learn Korean Phrases with K-Drama: "Go first"
description:
Today's expression is "먼저 가", which means "Go ahead" or "Go first" in English. It’s a polite and thoughtful phrase you can use when letting someone leave before you or proceed ahead of you.

📝 Breakdown

먼저 [meon·jeo]: first, beforehand
가다 [ga·da]: to go
가 [ga]: casual form
가요 [ga·yo]: polite form
가세요 [ga·se·yo]: honorific/polite request form


먼저 가 – casual (between close friends or younger people)
먼저 가요 – polite (for general respectful situations)
먼저 가세요 – more polite/honorific (to elders or strangers)

Use it when you want to show courtesy in everyday situations! 😉

title: 📺✨ Learn Korean Phrases with K-Drama: "later" #kdrama #shadowingkorean #korean
description:
Today's expression is "나중에," which means "later" in English. It’s a common and versatile word used in everyday Korean to refer to a future time, especially when you want to delay or postpone something. When used by itself—“나중에”—it can be a casual way to say you’ll do something later or talk about something another time.


📝 Breakdown
나중 [na·jung]: later, afterward
에 [e]: time particle (attached to indicate “at a later time”)


Example uses:

When someone invites you to do something and you're busy:
“나중에.” – “Later.”

When you don’t want to answer a question right now:
“나중에.” – “I’ll tell you later.”

title: Korean Phrases in K-dramas #korean #learnkorean #koreanlanguage #kdrama #한국어
description:
Today, we’re going to learn some expressions commonly used in Korean dramas! hese phrases will help you sound more natural and understand everyday conversations better. Let's get started!

1. 알겠어 (Al-ge-sseo) - Got it
2. 왜? (Wae?) - Why?
3. 진짜? (Jin-jja?) - Really?
4. 어떡해 (Eo-tteok-hae) - What should I do?
5. 야, 죽을래?! (Ya, ju-geul-lae?!) - Hey, do you want to die?
6. 하지마 (Ha/ji/ma) - Don't do that

Practice these phrases and try using them in different contexts. It will help you get a better grasp of Korean expressions and make your conversations more lively and natural!

title: 📺Learn Korean Phrases with K-Drama: "What do you like?"
description:
Today's expression is "뭐 좋아해?", which means "What do you like?" in English. This is a casual way to ask someone about their preferences — whether it’s food, music, hobbies, or anything else! You’ll often hear this in friendly conversations or in K-dramas when characters are getting to know each other.

📝 Breakdown  
뭐 [mwo]: what  
좋아해 [jo·a·hae]: like (casual form of 좋아하다)

Polite form: 뭐 좋아해요?

This phrase is useful when you're making conversation or trying to get closer to someone. Try using it with friends or someone you want to learn more about!

title: 📺Learn Korean Phrases with K-Drama: "I'm back", "I've returned"
description:
Today's expression is "다녀왔습니다," which can be translated as "I'm back" or "I've returned" in English. Koreans commonly say this phrase when they come back home, return to the office, or arrive back from somewhere.

📝 Breakdown

다녀오다 [da·nyeo·o·da]: to go somewhere and come back
다녀왔습니다: formal form of "다녀오다"

Together, 다녀왔습니다 literally means "I went and came back."


✨ Usage Tip

"다녀왔습니다" is more than just a sentence—it’s a common Korean greeting used when returning home or coming back to a place where people are waiting for you. By saying it, you're letting others know, "I'm back safely."

Interestingly, even though "다녀왔습니다" is a formal form, Koreans often use it at home regardless of age or relationship. It sounds natural and is commonly used as a set expression when arriving home.


I hope this video is interesting and helpful to you! 🐰🤗



📚 Want to understand more of what your favorite K-drama characters are really saying?

Learning expressions from dramas is fun, but building a strong grammar foundation will help you catch the nuances, understand conversations more naturally, and learn Korean faster.

title: 📺Learn Korean with K-Drama: 6 Question Words in Korean
description:
Today’s expressions are Korean question words that often stand alone as powerful emotional reactions. While these words are typically used to ask questions like "what", "who", "where", "when", "how", and "why", they are often used on their own — not only to ask real questions, but also to express strong emotions like surprise, confusion, or urgency, depending on the tone and context.

📝 Breakdown
  
뭐? [mwo]
What?

누구? [nu·gu] / 누가? [nu·ga]
Who? / Who? (subject)

어디? [eo·di] 
Where?

언제? [eon·je] 
When?

어떻게? [eo·tteo·ke]
How?

왜? [wae] 
Why?

These words are super useful whether you’re learning Korean or watching your favorite K-Dramas!

I hope this video is interesting and helpful to you! 🐰🤗

title: 📺Learn Korean Phrases with K-Drama: Forget it or It's fine
description:
Today's expression is "됐어," which translates to "Forget it" or "Never mind" in English. This phrase is commonly used when you want to dismiss something or tell someone to stop worrying about it. It’s often heard in K-Dramas when characters are annoyed, frustrated, or want to end a conversation. For a more polite version, use "됐어요."

Difference between '괜찮아' and '됐어':

괜찮아 [gwaen·chan·a]: This means "It's okay" or "I'm fine," and it’s used when reassuring someone or when you want to say that something is acceptable or no problem. It’s usually more positive and calming.

됐어 [dwaesseo]: This means "Forget it" or "Never mind." It’s used to dismiss something, often signaling that the speaker doesn’t want to continue talking about it. It has a more dismissive or indifferent tone compared to "괜찮아."

Breakdown:

되다 [doe·da]: to become, to be
됐어 [dwaesseo]: past tense form, meaning "It's fine" or "Forget it"
I hope this video helps you better understand Korean expressions! 🐰🤗

title: 📺Learn Korean Phrases with K-Drama: #1. Family Members in Korean
description:
Today's expressions are Korean family terms used to refer to older family members or people you are close to. These words are used according to the speaker's gender and the relationship with the person. These are essential when learning Korean because they’re used more widely than just within families — often in social settings too!

📝 Breakdown

- 엄마 [eom·ma]: mom
- 아빠 [a·ppa]: dad
- 누나 [nu·na]: older sister (used by males)
- 언니 [eon·ni]: older sister (used by females)
- 형 [hyeong]: older brother (used by males)
- 오빠 [o·ppa]: older brother (used by females)

Polite forms (when directly calling or referring politely):
- 어머니 [eo·meo·ni]: mother
- 아버지 [a·beo·ji]: father

Note: In Korean, using the correct family title based on your gender is important. These terms can also be used affectionately with close friends or people you respect, depending on context!

I hope this video is interesting and helpful to you! 🐰🤗

title: 📺Learn Korean Phrases with K-Drama: "I think that"
description:
Today’s expression is „내 생각에는,“ which means „In my opinion“ or „I think that“ in English. This phrase is often used when expressing your personal thoughts or opinions about a situation. It’s a common and polite way to introduce your perspective in a conversation.

📝 Breakdown
내 [nae]: my 
생각 [saeng·gak]: thought
에 [e]: a particle used to indicate „in“ or „as for“
는 [neun]: topic particle for emphasis

✨ If you want to be more polite or formal, you can use „제 생각에는“ [je saeng·ga·ge·neun], where „제“ is the humble form of „내“ (my), often used in respectful speech.

I hope this explanation helps you express your thoughts confidently! 💭✨

title: 📺✨ Learn Korean Phrases with K-Drama: "What are they saying?" or "What did they say?"
description:
Today's expression is "뭐래?" (casual) and "뭐래요?" (polite), which literally means "What are they saying?" or "What did they say?"

In conversation, 뭐래요? is usually used to ask what someone said in a neutral or polite way. However, 뭐래? is often used with a sarcastic or dismissive tone when reacting to something that sounds ridiculous, unreasonable, or hard to believe. In these situations, it can be understood as "What's that supposed to mean?", "What are they even talking about?", or "What nonsense are they saying?"

Because of this nuance, the sarcastic meaning is much more common with the casual form 뭐래? than with the polite form 뭐래요?.



📝 Breakdown

뭐 [mwo]: what
-래 [-rae]: contracted form related to -라고 해, meaning "someone said" or "they say"
요 [yo]: polite sentence ending



I hope this video is interesting and helpful to you! 🐰🤗

"Really?" or "Is that so? 📺✨Learn Korean Phrases with K-Drama: 그래? [geu·rae?]
description:
Today's expression is "그래?", which can be translated as "Really?" or "Is that so?" in English. This phrase is used when you're reacting to something someone has just said, often to show mild surprise or curiosity. It's a casual and versatile response, commonly used in everyday conversations.

📝 Breakdown 
그렇다 [geu·reo·ta]: to be so, to be like that
그래 [geu·rae]: the casual form of "그렇다," meaning "it is so"
➡️ 그래?: The rising intonation makes it a question, turning it into "Really?" or "Is that so?"

Polite form: 그래요?

I hope this helps you understand more Korean through K-Drama! 🎬🌟

In this video you can learn 50 very common and useful Korean questions with examples from many different Korean dramas. Note that most of these words and expressions are informal and would normally only be used with friends or family, but they are nevertheless used very often and you will hear them frequently in Korean. Also note that Korean and English are very different languages and it is not always easy to directly translate the meaning or pronunciation of a Korean word. 

For example, in Korean the "L" is often pronounced closer to an English rolling "R".
Also, when a Korean word starts with an "M" as in for instance [mwoya], the "M" is denasalized in Korean making it sound closer to an English "B". It's comparable to saying "mwoya" while pinching your nose or having a cold!
Similarly, the "N" at the beginning of Korean words often sounds closer to an English "D", as in for instance [nugu].

title: 📺Learn Korean Phrases with K-Drama: 7 Most Commonly Used Words by Koreans
description:
These 7 expressions were picked as some of the most frequently used phrases in everyday Korean conversation. You’ll hear them not only in K-dramas but also in daily chats all over Korea. Learn these to sound more natural and emotionally expressive in Korean!


📝 Breakdown

👉 아니 [a·ni]
Literally "no," but often used as a filler to start a sentence, especially when expressing surprise, frustration, or disbelief. It doesn't actually mean "no" in these cases — think of it like "Wait," "Seriously," or "Come on" in English.

👉 근데 [geun·de]
Short for "그런데," meaning "but" or "by the way." It’s used to smoothly contrast what was said before or to shift the topic. A very common way to connect thoughts in casual conversation.

👉 있잖아 [it·jja·na]
Used when trying to get someone's attention or start explaining something, similar to "You know..." or "So..." in English. It often signals that the speaker wants to say something important or personal.

👉 솔직히 [sol·jji·ki]
Means "honestly" or "to be frank." Used when you’re about to share your true thoughts or feelings. Often adds sincerity or a blunt tone to what follows.

👉 진짜 [jin·jja]
Literally means "really," but it's also used for strong emphasis. Depending on tone, it can show excitement, annoyance, disbelief, or seriousness — much like "Seriously" or "For real" in English.

👉 인간적으로 [in·gan·jeo·geu·ro]
Literally "as a human" or "humanly speaking," but often used as a filler to emphasize a point or appeal to someone's decency. In English, it's closer to "Seriously," "Come on," or "Be reasonable."

👉 약간 [yak·kkan]
Means "a bit" or "kind of." While it can describe small amounts, it's also commonly used as a filler to soften statements or make things sound less direct, much like "sort of" or "kinda" in English.


🎬 These everyday expressions show up constantly in K-dramas and real-life Korean conversations. Learn them, and you’ll instantly sound more natural and expressive in Korean! 🇰🇷🗣️

title: 📺Learn Korean Phrases with K-Drama: "Just a moment"
description:
Today's expression is "잠시만" or its polite form "잠시만요," which means "Just a moment" or "Excuse me for a second" in English. This phrase is used when you want someone to wait for a short moment, or when you're trying to politely get someone's attention or navigate through a crowd.

📝 Breakdown  
잠시 [jam·si]: a short moment  
만 [man]: only  

Polite form: 잠시만요  

You’ll hear this often in public places, like when someone tries to pass through or when asking someone to pause or wait briefly. It's a super useful expression for daily life in Korea!


👉 Difference between 잠시만 and 잠깐만

These two are often used interchangeably in casual conversations, but there’s a slight nuance difference!

- 잠시만 feels slightly more formal and can imply a bit longer pause. You might hear this more in service settings (like when a staff member asks you to wait).
- 잠깐만 sounds more casual and is often used among friends or when you’re asking someone to wait just a tiny moment.


I hope this video is interesting and helpful to you! 🐰🤗

title: 📺✨Learn Korean Phrases with K-Drama 힘내 [him-nae] (Formal: 힘내요 / 힘내세요)
description:
Today's expression is "힘내," which means "Cheer up!" or "Stay strong!" in English. This phrase is commonly used to encourage someone who is facing a challenge or going through a tough time. It’s an expression of support, reminding someone to keep going.

📝 Breakdown 
힘 [him]: strength, energy 
내다 [nae-da]: to make, to produce

⚠️ The difference between 화이팅 and 힘내
"화이팅" and "힘내" both mean "cheer up," but they’re used a bit differently. "화이팅" is like saying "You can do it!" and is used to cheer someone on before a challenge, such as an exam or a sports game. It’s upbeat and full of energy. On the other hand, "힘내" is closer to "Stay strong!" and is used to support someone going through a tough time. It’s a bit more comforting and often conveys encouragement during difficult situations. 

Use this phrase to uplift someone’s spirits when they need it! 🌟💪

title: EXO Destroyed by Korean Fire Noodle Challenge🔥🔥
description:
12 years ago, we started what would become one of the most viral challenges on YouTube, the fire noodle challenge. We’ve had people from around the world and every walk of life try it, except for Koreans. So today, we brought EXO's Chanyeol, Suho and Kai to see how they handle the spice!


12년 전 저희가 시작한 불닭볶음면 챌린지가 이렇게 커질 줄은 꿈에도 몰랐는데요. 남녀노소 할 것 없이 세계 곳곳 여러 사람들에게 소개했지만 정작 한국인과는 해본 적이 없었어요. 과연 엑소 분들이 한국인의 맵부심을 지켜낼 수 있을까요?

description:

Grab a warm snack, get cozy, and join us for a taste test full of winter vibes, laughter, and a lot of sticky fingers.

This is not a sponsored episode, but huge thanks to @angel_dabang and @hoho.ldn for making Korean snacks that you can now try in London!

⛄한국 곳곳에서 눈 소식이 들려오는데요. 겨울하면 빠질 수 없는 간식들을 총동원해 오랜만에 풀해남고를 찾아갔어요! ❄️ 여러분도 붕어빵 하나씩 들고 겨울 분위기 물씬내며 아이들의 솔직하고 귀여운 반응을 즐겨보세요.

광고는 아니지만 이번 영상에 나온 한국 간식을 이젠 런던에서도 손쉽게 만나볼 수 있다고 해요! 꽈배기는 @angel_dabang에서 그리고 호떡은 @hoho.ldn에서 맛보실 수 있습니다.

Today we're back with the British footballers to give them a break from World Cup mourning with some Korean Street Food Healing! ❤️

Huge thank you to @HashtagUnited for giving their time and letting us film with them again! It's always a pleasure! If you want to see behind the scenes from this episode head over to Hashtag united's channel here! https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kyIODCXxVpE

--

오늘은 영국 축구 선수들과 함께 월드컵 시청을 잠깐 멈추고 한국 길거리 음식을 먹어봤어요! ❤️

title: British Highschoolers try Korean Viral Desserts (ft.Dubai Chewy Chocolate)
description:
🌏 Get an exclusive 15% discount on Saily data plans! Use code KEMAN at checkout. Download Saily app or go to https://saily.com/keman ⛵️

British high schoolers are back… and this time they’re diving into Korea’s most viral desserts! 🇰🇷 🍰
From the nostalgic sweetness of Injeolmi Toast to the infamous Dubai Chewy Cookie, we’re taking them on a journey through the desserts that have taken Korea by storm 🤩

Huge thanks to @shibuya.soho for the croffles @nagarelondon for the salt bread and @haydaybake for the Dubai chewy cookies!

🌏 Saily 데이터 플랜 결제하실 때 KEMAN 코드를 입력하시면 15% 할인을 받으실 수 있습니다! Saily앱 다운로드는 링크를 확인하세요: https://saily.com/keman ⛵️

영국 고등학생들이 돌아왔습니다! 이번에는 한국에서 한동안 유행했던 디저트들 다 모아봤는데요 🇰🇷 🍰
쫀득하고 고소한 인절미 토스트부터 전국을 흔든 두쫀쿠까지 정복한 영국 고딩들의 반응 많이 기대해주세요 🤩

오늘 영상에 나온 한국 간식들 런던에서 맛 보실 수 있습니다! 크로플은 @shibuya.soho 소금빵은 @nagarelondon 그리고 두쫀쿠는 @haydaybake에서 찾으실 수 있습니다.

title: British Highschoolers try 11 types of Korean Fried Chicken!!
description:
#순살만공격
This week, we are taking the boys out for Korean Fried Chicken which they've been wanting for a long time. But not just some chicken, the boys are about to try 11 different types of chicken...

Thanks to #순살만공격 for their delicious 11 types of chicken and sponsoring this episode.

--

오늘 에피소드에서는 학생들을 데리고 그들이 그토록 원하던 한국 치킨을 먹으러 나갔답니다. 하지만 그냥 치킨이 아니라, 차원이 다른 11가지 다른 맛의 치킨을 맛보여주고 싶어 순살만공격에 방문 했어요!

오늘 영상에서 영국 고등학생들이 맛 본 프리미엄 순살치킨이 궁금하다면 순살만공격.com을 방문해보세요!

title: British Uni Students try everything at a Korean Convenience Store!!
description:
Today we take Armand and Max to a Korean convenience store and let them get whatever they want to try! How many baskets did they fill up? Watch till the end to find out!
Next video comes out on Saturday!

This video isn't sponsored but thank you to GS25 for helping us film this episode!

--

오늘은 믿기 힘들겠지만 아르망과 맥스가 처음으로 한국 편의점에 가봅니다!! 갑자기 수정과에 꽂힌 아르망과 삼각김밥과 씨름 시작한 맥스..!! 오늘 무사히 집에 갈 수 있겠죠?
다음 영상은 토요일에!

오늘 영상은 광고가 아님을 알려드립니다. 촬영에 협조해주신 GS25에 감사의 말씀을 전합니다!

title: Why I left Jolly and Korean Englishman
description:
Dear Josh and Ollie,

This was so corny for me to make, but honestly I thought why not haha. 
I really appreciate and love the amount of time we made some of the best content that I happened in the US and more. I still remember the day I was so burnt out of working withother YouTubers, but you guys reignited the spark for me. Words cannot express how grateful I am to have such great friends/boss/influence/role models. I really wish to see you guys more often and if you're ever in the US. I'd love to help anytime with a heart beat. To the rest of the team, especially Adam, nothing but love for you really believing in me and giving me a chance to just spend life and read bible with you everyday. Hope I will make you proud with whatever I do next

Love you guys,
Ducky

Here's how to support me:
https://ko-fi.com/duckybokki

title: 편의점 꿀조합을 처음 먹어본 영국 고등학생들의 반응!? (ft.짜파구리 마크정식)
description:
인스타그램으로 편의점 음식 메뉴를 추천해주신 분들, 그리고 오늘 영상의 삼각김밥 만들어준  @gabiekook 에게 감사를 전합니다!

영상의 모든 스태프 전원은 촬영 이전 코로나19 음성 확진을 받았습니다.
모든 촬영 과정은 정부의 방역 지침에 따라 전문가의 확인을 받아 진행되었습니다.
#영국고딩 #편의점음식 #반응

--

Big thanks to all of you on Instagram who sent in suggestions for which Korean convenience store food to include, and of course to  @gabiekook  who helped us prepare those incredible triangle kimbabs (that otherwise are impossible to find in the UK!) for the boys to try.

All of the crew had a negative COVID-19 test prior to shooting.
All activities were fully risk assessed and monitored in accordance with UK government guidelines.

title: SHINee MINHO + 3 bowls of Korean Pig's Blood Sausage!!
description:
Today we meet SHINee Minho, man of seemingly endless talents, but also one of the most humble and kind guests we’ve ever had on the show!
And he takes us out to his favourite Sundae (Korean Pig’s Blood) Soup restaurant!!

Minho's first album "CHASE" is out now! Go check it out!
https://youtu.be/UaWcBFYKTT4

--

오늘은 다재다능하면서 친절하고 멋지고 겸손하기 까지하신 샤이니의 민호씨를 만났는데요! 민호씨의 최애 순댓국밥집에 가서 식사를 하며 대화하는 시간을 가졌습니다!

민호님의 첫 엘범 "CHASE" 도 한번 들어보세요!
https://youtu.be/UaWcBFYKTT4

title: British Students go to Korean Navy Convenience Store! (ultimate boot camp reward)
description:
Get 4 months extra on a 2 year plan here: https://nordvpn.com/keman. It’s risk free with Nord’s 30 day money-back guarantee!  
Today we take the British Uni boys to PX, aka military convenience store. From combinations of different noodles to special chicken, dumplings, and pizza unique to these military bases, the Korean soldiers do know what they’re doing when it comes to food combinations. What was their favourite? Watch till the end to find out!

https://nordvpn.com/keman  으로 회원가입 하시면 저희 추천코드로 최대 4개월까지 할인받으실 수 있답니다! 노드에서는 30일 이내 환불 보장까지 해준답니다!
오늘은 훈련을 마친 영국 대학생 친구들과 함께, PX를 갑니다! 짧은 일정이지만 군대 안에서최대한 많은 걸 경험하게 해주고 싶었는데요. 과연, 현역 군인분들이 직접 추천, 만들어주신 요즘 PX 꿀조합을 영국 대학생들은 어떻게 생각할까요? 이번 영상도 재밌게 봐주셨음 좋겠네요.

title: British College Students get SHY in front of K-POP Girl Group fromis_9!!
description:
Straight from the military, we take the boys through a rather extreme change of environment: KPOP Headquarters. We meet with Korean soldiers’ favourite girl group, fromis_9 to learn their dance (and see if any of us can join their group to form fromis_10)! Watch the video to find out who was the best and worst! We then take the boys for a relaxing evening at a Korean cinema, trying all types of Korean cinema snacks and watching a film on ScreenX (https://bit.ly/ScreenX_Eng)! Hope you enjoy today’s episode as well!

--


오늘은 군대에서 갓 서울로 복귀한 영국 대학생들을 데리고, 조금 급격한 변화를 겪어봅니다. 바로 KPOP성지 하이브에서 프로미스나인과 함께 시간을 보내게 되는데요, 안무도 직접 가르쳐주시고 가장 좋아하는 배달음식도 시켜서 같이 밥도 먹고, 정말 즐거운 시간을 보냈답니다. 시간 내어주신 프로미스나인 멤버분들, PLEDIS Entertainment 관계자분들께 감사인사드립니다.  그리고 영상 끝에선 그동안 고생한 아이들을 위해 영화관 가서 힐링하는 시간을 갖는데요, 한국 영화관 간식들, 그리고 초대형 스크린X (https://bit.ly/ScreenX_KR) 를 처음 본 영국학생들 반응은 어땠을까요?ㅎㅎ 이번 영상도 재밌게 봐주셨음 좋겠네요^^

title: Italian Chef tries the BEST Street Market food in Korea!!
description:
After trying some of the fanciest, most expensive food in Korea, we took Mr. Z to try some of the best, most authentic street market food Korea has to offer. $5 hand cut noodles prepared down a local alley by a Korean Grandma.

A big thank you to Kia EV9 for sponsoring this video!

This isn't an ad for the restaurant but let's support local businesses!
Kim Kyeong Ja Wonjo Son Kalguksu Bossam:
394 Geumho-dong 3(sam)-ga, Seongdong-gu, Seoul, South Korea

----

오늘은 다시 Mr. Z의 한국 맛집 탐방으로 돌아왔는데요! 그동안 럭셔리한 한국 특식들을 경험했다면 이번엔 현지인들만 아는 찐 맛집, 할머니 손맛을 제대로 느낄 수 있는 곳으로 갔습니다! 파스타와 생면을 먹고 자란 이태리 셰프 Mr. Z는 할머니 손 찰 국수에 대해 어떻게 생각할까요?

오늘 영상을 제작할 수 있도록 지원해주신 Kia EV9에 감사의 말씀을 드립니다!

광고는 아니지만 지역상인분들을 서포트 합니다!
김경자원조 손칼국수 보쌈: 서울 성동구 금호산2길 20-1

title: British Highschoolers try Korean Pizza Delivery for the first time!
description:
Today we are taking the highschoolers to Han riverside for a cha-c-nic. We thought it would be a perfect chance for them to try Korean pizza delivery!

오늘은 영국 고딩들을 데리고 한강에 왔어요. 차크닉을 준비해 우리 고딩들이 한국의 편리한 배달 문화를 경험하게 해줬어요!
이 영상에 도움을 주신 기아자동차와 반올림피자샵에 감사드립니다.

반올림피자샵에서 영국남자 구독자들을 위해 준비한 3천원 할인 이벤트를 확인 해보세요! 
https://www.banolimpizza.com/main
쿠폰번호 : 영국남자단짠단짠

title: 한우 + 치즈 조합 처음 먹어보고 영국 못 갈 뻔한 데이비드 베컴?!🤭 (형님 푸파셨군요..!!😍)
description:
#데이비드베컴 #한우 #Ad #DavidBeckham
이번 인터뷰를 가능하게 해 주시고, 영상 제작에 후원해주신 Adidas Korea에 감사드립니다!
오늘은 아마 세계에서 가장 유명한 영국남자이자 저와 올리의 인생 영웅인 데이비드 베컴님과 한우를 먹어봅니다!! 인터뷰를 호텔 안에서 진행하게 되어서 저희가 제일 좋아하는 식당 사장님께 부탁해서 제일 좋은 고기와 반찬들을 준비해 갔는데 아직까지 꿈인지 생신지 헷갈릴 정도로 너무 떨리고 행복한 인터뷰였어요! 너무 긴장돼서 말도 제대로 안 나왔었는데 여유 넘치고 재치 넘치시는 베컴님 덕분에 정줄 붙들고 인터뷰 마칠 수 있었답니다.😂 한우, 된장찌개, 그리고 한우 + 치즈 조합까지 폭풍 흡입하신 데이비드 베컴님의 첫 유튜브 한우 먹방...!! 재밌게 봐주셨음 좋겠네요!!

한우 준비해주신 사장님께도 감사드립니다!
여의도 생고기
서울 영등포구 의사당대로1길 34

Today we meet the legendary Englishman, our childhood hero, David Beckham and treat him to top quality Korean beef!! We picked up the meat from the best Hanwoo restaurant we know in Seoul and took it to him! We still can’t believe this actually happened! I was so nervous I forgot to introduce him to our favourite combination to eat with beef until David eyed it up and asked me about it! 😂 It was such an honour to meet him and film a mukbang with him, and we really hope you guys enjoy this episode!!

Also a huge thank you to the manager at "Yeoeuido Saenggogi" for preparing the Hanwoo for us!
Address: 34 Uisadang-daero 1-gil, Yeoeuido-dong, Seoul

Huge thanks to everyone involved in the creation of this video:
Written and Directed by Josh and Ollie
Producer/Editor: Grace Park
Producer/Editor/Translator: Hyemin Lim
Editor: Sejun Lee
Translator: Hannah Kang

title: 치맥을 처음 먹어본 영국 축구선수들의 반응?!
description:
작년에 영국 축구팀에게 한국 바베큐를 소개 했었는데요. 이번엔 축구하면 떠오르는 한국 치킨을 소개 하고 싶었어요. 런던에서 저희가 가장 좋아하는 치킨 가게와 가비의 도움으로 만든 치킨을 들고 경기장으로 갔답니다.
저희에게 시간을 내주시고 촬영에 흔쾌히 응해주신 해시태그 유나이티드에 감사드립니다. 

여기서 해시태그 유나이티드의 모습을 보실 수 있어요: https://www.youtube.com/user/spencerbets

치킨을 신선하고 완벽하게 준비하는데 도와준 가비에게 감사합니다! 
가비 채널은 여기에서 볼 수 있어요: https://www.youtube.com/user/gkook1

오리지널 치킨과 소스는 이태원 버거에서, 그리고 치킨과 매운 볼케이노 소스는 페리카나에서 공수했습니다.
#영국축구 #축구선수 #치맥

--

Last year we introduced Korean BBQ to this British football team, and this year, with the help of Gabie and our favourite chicken shop in the UK, we brought them my favourite food to have after a good game of footie: Korean Fried Chicken!

Huge thank you to Hashtag United for giving us their time and letting us film. Go check them out here:
https://www.youtube.com/user/spencerbets

Big thanks as well to Gabie for helping us prepare the chicken fresh and perfectly for them! Check out her channel here: https://www.youtube.com/user/gkook1

We got the original chicken and sauces from Itaewon Burger and Chicken and the spicy Volcano sauce from Pelicana.

title: 수능 영어를 풀어본 영국 고등학생들…!!?
description:
오늘은 고등학생들과 유명한 수능 문제들을 풀어보러 풀햄고로 돌아왔습니다! 잠시나마 다른 사람의 자리에서 수능 문제를 풀어보기로 해준 영국 고등학생분들께 감사드립니다!!
저희가 수능 영상을 몇 번 찍었는데, 매번 이 시기를 보내는 학생 여러분의 마음을 기억할 수 있는 좋은 기회인 것 같아요.
다음주 수능 보시는 모든 분들께 졸리와 영국남자팀 모두가 사랑을 담아 응원을 보냅니다! 기도할게요!

영상의 모든 출연진을 포함한 스태프 전원은 촬영 이전 코로나19 음성 확진을 받았습니다.
모든 촬영 과정은 정부의 방역 지침에 따라 전문가의 확인을 받아 진행되었습니다.

--

Today we’re back at the school, this time to take a look at the notorious Korean SAT. Huge thanks to all the boys who were willing to put themselves in someone else’s shoes today and try this exam!!
We’ve done a few videos about this huge exam in Korea before, and every year I think it’s a great opportunity to remember what so many people are going through right now.
For everyone in Korea about to take the KSAT next week we want to send love and support from the whole KoreanEnglishman and JOLLY team. We’ll be praying for you!

Everyone in this video including the crew had a negative COVID-19 test prior to shooting.
All activities were fully risk assessed and monitored in accordance with UK government guidelines.

title: British Rappers shocked by Crazy Korean night market!
description:
Today we take the British rappers to Seoul's famous shopping district, Myeongdong to explore the amazing night market! 

Thank you to HBAF https://bit.ly/3KNRqSO for sponsoring this video.
If you would also like to visit their flagship store, this is where they are!
📍HBAF Myeongdon: https://bit.ly/41gVWP8

And a special thanks to our series sponsor OBC!

--

오늘은 영국 래퍼들과 서울에 오면 빠질 수 없는 명동으로 갔습니다! 다양하고 맛있는 야시장 음식을 점령해봤는데, 눈도 배도 아주 즐거운 시간이었어요!

오늘 영상 제작을 위해 협조해주신 HBAF (https://bit.ly/3KNRqSO) 에 감사드립니다. 영상에서 방문한 명동 HBAF 매장의 위치가 궁금하신 분들은 아래 링크를 확인해주세요.
📍HBAF 명동 : https://bit.ly/41gVWP8

title: We tried Michelin Star Korean Fried Chicken
description:
Going to therapy is a sign of strength, not weakness. BetterHelp makes therapy simple, with 10% off your first month to help you get started: 
https://betterhelp.com/koreanenglishman (Paid Partnership by BetterHelp)

Today we’re at Jeju Noodle Bar, one of the most sought after restaurant reservations in New York City, to talk to Chef Douglas Kim and taste the menu at the only Michelin Star Noodle Bar in America.

Huge thanks to Chef Kim and the rest of the Jeju Noodle Bar team for taking such good care of us and letting us film at their incredible restaurant.

상담을 받는다는 것은 약해서가 아니라 강해서 가능한 것이죠. BetterHelp를 통해 첫 달 상담료 10%를 할인받고 지금 시작해보세요: 
https://betterhelp.com/koreanenglishman (BetterHelp와의 유료 파트너십을 포함하고 있습니다.)

오늘은 뉴욕에서 예약하기 정말 어렵다는 Jeju Noodle Bar에서 더글러스 킴 셰프님 만나 뵙고 미국에서 하나뿐인 미슐랭 누들 바에서 여러 가지 메뉴를 맛보았답니다.

촬영에 흔쾌히 응해주신 더글러스 킴 셰프님과 Jeju Noodle Bar 스태프분들에게 감사드립니다.

title: British College Students try Korean Chicken BBQ + Fried Rice rolls!
description:
Today we take the boys on a road trip to Gangwon State! There are so many beautiful regions in Korea and it’d be a shame not to make the most of the time we have with the boys in Korea! So we thought we’d introduce them to a part of a Korean uni culture called “MT” and show them around beautiful cities in Gangwon State while we’re at it! First stop: good food/ chicken barbecue!!

--

오늘은 영국대학생 친구들과 엠티를 떠납니다! 한국에는 지역 구석구석 정말 아름다운 곳들이 많은데요, 다 같이 한국에 있는 시간동안 최대한 좋은 경험을 많이 하게 해주고 싶어서 바람도 쐴겸 강원특별자치도로 떠납니다! 우선 엠티의 첫번째 정류장, 춘천 닭갈비부터 홍천 알파카 월드까지..!! 이번 영상도 재밌게 봐주시구 다음주 수요일 영상도 기대해주세요!! #강원특별자치도 #호수문화권

title: British Uni Students get a job at a Korean Convenience Store!
description:
After trying almost every snack they sell, we surprised Armand and Max with the news that like a true student they are going to get a part time job at a Korean Convenience Store too!!

If you're in Korean, find a photosim booth and take a photo with us using the Korean Englishman photo-frame. We would love to see all the photos so do tag us on instagram!

This video isn't sponsored but thank you to GS25 for helping us film this episode!

--

그토록 가보고 싶었던 편의점에서 거의 모든 아이템을 먹어본 후 아르망과 맥스에게 특별한 서프라이즈를 준비했는데요. 한국 대학생이라면 한번쯤 해봤을 편의점 알바를 해보게했습니다!

근처 포토이즘에서 영국남자 프레임으로 저희와 함께 사진 찍어요! 저희도 사진들 볼 수 있게 저희 인스타에 태그 해주세요!

광고는 아니지만 촬영에 협조해주신 GS25에 감사의 말씀을 전합니다!

title: British Chefs Try Korean Delicacies! (ft. Fallow)
description:
Get Exclusive NordVPN deal + 4 months extra here: https://nordvpn.com/keman It’s risk-free with Nord’s 30-day money-back guarantee!


Our favorite British chefs are back, and this time, things are getting a little more intense! Last time, they loved classic Korean street food… but how will they react to some of Korea’s most unique and adventurous delicacies like spicy chicken feet and grilled pork intestines?


Check out Jack and Will's channel @FallowChefs  for more amazing culinary content.

Huge thanks to the team at Hongdae Pocha for letting us film this wonderful meal.
https://hongdaepocha.com

-

NordVPN 플랜에다 4개월을 무료로 추가해준다니 https://nordvpn.com/keman 에서 확인해보세요!
이번엔 영국 셰프님들을 모시고 이번엔 좀더 업그레이드가 된 음식들을 준비해봤는데요. 분식을 많이 좋아해주셨지만 과연 한국인들 사이에서도 호불호가 많이 갈리는 닭발이나 곱창 같은 안주에 대한 어떤 반응을 보여주실까요?


잭과 윌의 채널인 @FallowChefs 에서 재미난 요리 영상들을 보실 수 있습니다.

흔쾌히 촬영에 응해주시고 맛있는 음식을 대접해주신 홍대 포차 스태프 분들께 감사드립니다.
https://hongdaepocha.com

title: 엄마랑 한국 수산시장 가서 제일 큰 킹크랩 달라고 했더니...!!!😱😍
description:
저희 엄마가 해산물을 정말 좋아하세요. 그래서 엄마를 모시고 한국 수산시장에 가서, 제가 본 것 중 제일 큰 킹크랩을 먹어봤어요!

My mum loves seafood. And so today we take my mum to a Korean Seafood market, and buy the biggest king crab I have ever seen!!!!

Huge thanks to everyone involved in the creation of this video:
Written and Directed by Josh and Ollie
Producer/Editor: Grace Park
Producer/Editor/Translator: Hyemin Lim
Editor/Translator: Sejun Lee
Editor: Sepi Zolfaghari
Translator: Hannah Kang

title: When British Geniuses Try Korean Heat    PANIC 😱
NCT 인터뷰 비공개 풀영상 드디어 공개합니다...!!
description:
저희가 그동안 특별한 연예인 인터뷰를  많이 해왔는데요, 주로 한국에 오신 해외 스타분들께 처음으로 한국 음식을 소개하는 방식이었죠 (https://youtu.be/DQWUAb-hmlg). 그런데 특히 아이돌 그룹 NCT의 마크님과 재현님과 함께한 인터뷰는 정말 역대급으로 즐거웠던 인터뷰였답니다. 저희의 인터뷰를 보고 많은 분들께서 무편집본이나 더 긴 영상을 올려달라는 요청을 계속해서 해주셔서, 저희 최애 인터뷰들 중 3개를 그렇게 공개하기로 했답니다! 첫 번째 영상으로 저희가 NCT 멤버분들과 함께한 (사실상) 무편집 영상을 준비했어요!
다음 영상은 이번주 토요일에 공개될 예정이니 많이 기대해주세요!

We’ve done some pretty special celeb interviews, often introducing celebrities visiting Korea to Korean food for the first time (https://youtu.be/DQWUAb-hmlg), but one of our favourites was this interview with KPOP stars Mark and Jaehyun from NCT. For many of these interviews you guys have asked us over and over again to upload uncut extended versions of the interviews, so for 3 of our favourites, we’ve got just that for you! Here’s the first: our full interview with NCT uncut (basically!) and fully subtitled. 

#NCT127 #NCT #SuperM

The next one will be uploaded this Saturday, so stay tuned!
Enjoy.

Huge thanks to everyone involved in the creation of this video:
Written and Directed by Josh and Ollie
Producer/Editor: Grace Park
Producer/Editor/Translator: Hyemin Lim
Editor: Sejun Lee
Translator: Hannah Kang

title: K-Pop Idol NCT Johnny fed us luxury Korean Beef BBQ!!
description:
Today we’re back in Korea for the first time since getting back from the school trip, and we’re meeting our old friends NCT!! Last time a bunch of you told us that next time we need to meet Johnny too, so we took your advice!

Huge thanks to Johnny for a great time and being such a gent!

This is the first of a few episodes where we get the chance to sit down with some top Korean artists over some incredible Korean food and bring you guys along for the journey!

--

오늘은 오랜만에 한국에 다시 돌아와 NCT의 쟈니님를 만났습니다! 많은 분들이 저번에 NCT 마크님과 재현님과 촬영했을 때 쟈니님도 꼭 만나야 봐야 한다고 얘기해 주셔서 이번에 이렇게 식사를 같이 하게 됐어요!

재밌는 시간을 함께 만들어주신 쟈니님께 감사의 말씀을 전합니다!

이번 영상은 저희가 여러 한국 아티스트 분들과 함께 한국 음식을 먹으면서 얘기 나누는 시리즈 중 첫 영상인데요. 앞으로 나올 영상들 많이 기대해 주세요!

title: 한국 치킨을 처음 먹어본 영국 고등학생들의 반응!
description:
많은 분들이 댓글 달아주신데로… 오늘은 영국 고등학생들에게 한국 치킨을 소개했습니다!

---

As many have you requested… Today we gave the British high schoolers Korean 
Chicken!

title: When you get the lyrics wrong in front of the actual artist 😬 (ft. Minho from SHINee)
British Highschoolers Fly to Korea to try REAL Street Food!!
description:
A year after a handshake and a promise… the British Highschoolers have finally come on a School Trip to Korea! 7 students. 3 teachers. Months of planning with our whole crew. And a trip full of life-changing, unforgettable new experiences. Friendship, bonding, and young men experiencing and then falling in love with a whole new culture.
We hope you enjoy the whole series - stay tuned, there's more to come!!!

Special thanks to Masung Tteokbokki for sponsoring this episode and helping us to make this trip as special as possible!
http://www.masungfood.com/

--

한국에 데려가겠단 약속을 한지 1년… 영국 고등학생들이 드디어 수학여행으로 한국에 왔습니다! 7명의 학생들. 3분의 선생님들. 잊지 못할 추억과 인생 일대의 경험을 하며 쌓은 우정. 그리고 색다른 문화를 보고 사랑에 빠진 청년들.
지금까지 봐주신 여러분들 덕분에 가능했습니다. 앞으로 매주 올라올 저희 고등학생들의 수학여행 시리즈 즐겁게 봐주세요!

학생들에게 맛있는 분식을 소개할 수 있도록 이 에피소드에 도움을 주신 마성떡볶이에 감사드립니다.
http://www.masungfood.com/

title: British Chefs try Korean Street Food for the first time! (ft. Fallow)
description:
Going to therapy is a sign of strength, not weakness. BetterHelp makes therapy simple, with 10% off your first month to help you get started:
https://betterhelp.com/koreanenglishman (Paid Partnership by BetterHelp)

Last time we took Jack and Will (@FallowChefs ) for Korean BBQ, we discovered they didn’t have much experience with Korean food. So today, we brought some classic Korean street food straight to their restaurant, FOWL, in London.

Thank you to "Bunsik" for providing the food for us!


상담을 받는다는 것은 약해서가 아니라 강해서 가능한 것이죠. BetterHelp를 통해 첫 달 상담료 10%를 할인받고 지금 시작해보세요:

지난 번엔 잭과 윌에게 한국식 바베큐를 소개해 줬는데요. 이번엔 잭과 윌의 레스토랑인 FOWL에 저희가 직접 한국 분식을 들고가 영국 셰프들에게 소개해 줬답니다.

음식을 제공해주신 "분식"에 감사의 말씀을 드립니다!

title: British Rappers Try Korean Spicy Cold Noodles!
description:
Today we introduce British rappers to Korea's favourite Summer food, Naengmyeon (cold noodles)!

--

오늘은 Guvna B 와 Barney Artist 의 래퍼 친구들에게 여름의 별미 냉면을 소개 합니다! 

오늘 영상에서 소개된 열정국밥의 새로운 브랜드 해짱냉면은 잠실 방이동에 오픈한 플래그십스토어와 배달의민족 앱을 통해 만나보실 수 있습니다. http://naver.me/5DbGYRo1

title: British Rappers try Korean Fish & Chips!
The look on their faces 😍🇰🇷
Italian Chef tries REAL Korean food for the First Time?!
description:
Today we finally take our Italian chef friend, Mr.Z for Korean food and drinks! Some of you may know Mr. Z from our Jolly series where he showed us the BEST foods around Italy. Mr. Z has cooked for many big names like Beyonce, Kanye West, Drake, Anne Hathaway, Queen Elizabeth II, and now King Charles! This was a long overdue meal, but we actually had been saving it to bring some exciting news...  👀

This is not a sponsor let's support local businesses!
Thanks to Ogam Tapas Bar for taking care of us!
10 Chapel Market, London N1 9EZ
--

오늘은 드디어! 저희 이탈리아 셰프 친구 Mr. Z를 한국 식당으로 초대했습니다! 몇몇 분들은 Mr. Z를 저희 다른 채널 JOLLY에서 보셨을 텐데요. Mr. Z는 비욘세, 카녜 웨스트, 드레이크, 앤 해서웨이, 엘리자베스 2세 여왕 등 유명 인사들을 위해 요리를 해온 프라이빗 셰프인데요. 저희가 오랫동안 약속했었던 한식을 드디어 소개해서 먹었는데... 사실 아주 좋은 소식을 전하기 위해 좀 아껴왔었던 만남이었습니다...

광고는 아니지만 지역상인들을 늘 응원합니다!
오감 타파스 바: 10 Chapel Market, London N1 9EZ

title: Harvey (funny) moments in Korean Englishman 🪖😂
영국 고등학생들에게 불닭볶음면을 줘봤더니…?ㅎ🔥🥵 얘들아 미안…;;🚽
description:
7년전, 한국에 있는 여러분들이 저희에게 불닭볶음면이라는 새로운 라면을 보내주셨어요. 저희는 영국 친구들과 그 라면을 먹는 반응 영상을 찍었는데, 이게 전세계적으로 하는 바이럴 챌린지가 될줄은 꿈에도 몰랐어요. 그리고 오늘은 고등학생들과 초심으로 한번 돌아가봅니다…

영국고딩들이랑 한국 처음 가보는 시리즈 보고싶다면 영상 좋아요 눌러주시고 코멘트 남겨주세요~

영상의 모든 스태프 전원은 촬영 이전 코로나19 음성 확진을 받았습니다.
모든 촬영 과정은 정부의 방역 지침에 따라 전문가의 확인을 받아 진행되었습니다.
#영국고딩 #불닭 #라면

--

7 years ago you guys in Korea sent us a brand new instant noodle to try: Fire Noodles. We filmed a video giving them to our friends, and little did we know that would be the start of an international viral challenge. So today, we’re going back to our roots…

If you want to see us take the boys to Korea someday then hit like on the video and comment below! 

All of the crew had a negative COVID-19 test prior to shooting.
All activities were fully risk assessed and monitored in accordance with UK government guidelines.

title: I Tried Korean Englishman (영국남자) Toast in London
description:
@londonoli is not 😜😏 expect the line to be super long 👯‍♀️👯‍♂️ it will open from Wed - Sat, 12pm - 3pm

@koreanenglishman Josh and Ollie opened a Korean toast 🥪shop in @coaldropsyard near Kings 👑Cross Station 🚉 @koreantoastlondon 

They serve a similar version of the famous @_issac_toast . I remember eating in grad school in the morning and now seeing it in London was awesome. 

✨a little thought ✨The whole idea behind Korean toast is supposed to be super cheap and fast cause it is just for a quick grab 🏃‍♀️

Of course there are now premium sandwiches like @eggdrop.official in Korea, But I guess anything in London is expensive 💰💸 

the toast is about £4 - £6.5. But it is still tasty and a bit premium taste than #issactoast 🥪

#영국남자 #이삭토스트 #이삭토스트🍞 #londonkoreanfood #londonfoo

title: 한국 길거리 토스트 먹어 본 특별게스트들 반응은!? 🤭
description:
이번주 금요일 저희가 런던 킹스크로스역 옆에 Granary Square에 한국식 길거리 토스트 키오스크를 엽니다! 더 자세한 정보는 인스타그램에 @KoreanToastLondon 에서 알 수 있습니다. http://instagram.com/koreantoastlondon

그리고 오늘 영상에 출연한 유튜버들이 궁금하시다면 아래 링크를 클릭하시면 됩니다:
Sorted Food: www.youtube.com/SORTEDFood
Tom Scott: www.youtube.com/TomScottGo
Taz: www.youtube.com/ClickForTaz

완벽한 길거리 토스트 메뉴를 개발 해 준 가비  @국가비 Gabiekook  에게 너무나도 감사합니다! 저희 옆에서 많은 도움을 주고 저희의 상상을 현실로 만들어준 앨린과 케이트에게도 감사해요!

저희와 앨린과 케이트는 김치에 대한 열정과 또 그걸 전세계에 소개 시켜주고 싶은 마음이 항상 같았는데, 마침 그녀들이 굉장히 접하기 쉬운 김치를 개발했어요! 냉장고에 남은 김치를 보관할 필요없이 한 번에 먹기에 딱 좋은 양이더라고요! 해서 저희는 이 김치를 이용한 토스트 메뉴를 개발했답니다..!
피키위키 김치를 더 알고 싶다면: https://www.pickywicky.com

너무도 감사하게 죙 핫소스(@janghotsauce) 가 저희 가게를 후원해 주셨어요! 죙 핫소스의 감칠맛 나는 전통적인 한국 고추장 베이스 덕분에 한국의 매운맛을 저희 매운 토스트롤 통해 완벽하게 보여드릴 수 있게 됐었답니다!! :)
죙 핫소스를 더 알고 싶다면: https://bit.ly/3l3OWSV
영상의 모든 스태프 전원은 촬영 이전 코로나19 음성 확진을 받았습니다.
모든 촬영 과정은 정부의 방역 지침에 따라 전문가의 확인을 받아 진행되었습니다.

영상의 모든 출연진을 포함한 스태프 전원은 촬영 이전 코로나19 음성 확진을 받았습니다.
모든 촬영 과정은 정부의 방역 지침에 따라 전문가의 확인을 받아 진행되었습니다.
--
We opened a Korean Street Toast Restaurant in London!!
We’re launching our Korean Street Toast kiosk in Granary Square, Kings Cross this Friday! Follow @KoreanToastLondon on instagram for all the info. http://instagram.com/koreantoastlondon

Go check out the YouTubers who came to try our toast here:
Sorted Food: www.youtube.com/SORTEDFood
Tom Scott: https://www.youtube.com/TomScottGo
Taz: www.youtube.com/ClickForTaz

They all make brilliant content so go show them some love!
Huge thanks to  @국가비 Gabiekook for helping us develop the perfect street toast menu, and to Alin and Kate for supporting us and helping make this dream a reality!

Alin and Kate share our kimchi-obsession, and our desire to share it world-wide - so they developed the kimchi we used in our menu specifically to be as accessible as possible - perfect one-portion sizes without having to worry about storing and stinking out the rest of your fridge!
Go check out Picky Wicky Kimchi here: https://www.pickywicky.com

And thanks to Jang Hot Sauce (@janghotsauce)  for sponsoring our restaurant! We wanted an authentic Korean spice in our spicy toast, and Jang Hot Sauce has a delicious traditional Korean Gochujang base. Perfect!
Go check out Jang Hot Sauce here: https://bit.ly/3l3OWSV

All of the crew had a negative COVID-19 test prior to shooting.
All activities were fully risk assessed and monitored in accordance with UK government guidelines.

title: This is the #1 Korean BBQ in the World.
description:
Today we go to the restaurant that has just won "Best Korean BBQ" 1st place for the second year running, to taste the best of the best of Korean Hanwoo BBQ.

Hanwoo has been called "the forbidden beef", because it is impossible to get anywhere outside of Korea. Arguably the highest quality Beef in the world, we flew our friend Mr Z, an Italian Chef, all the way to Korea to try it.

This is the first episode of a 6 past series - searching out the very best food Korea has to offer.

This is not a sponsor but thank you to Chef Min Kyeong Hwan and to Born & Bred for giving us an amazing experience!
--


한우는 세계 최고의 품질을 자랑하는 소고기입니다. 그렇지만 한국에서만 먹을 수 있죠. 보인다! 왔어! 그래서 오늘은 저희 친구이자 세계 탑 레스토랑에서 요리를 하고, 탑 할리우드 연예인들에게 음식을 해준 이탈리아 셰프 Mr.Z 를 한국에 초대해서 한국에서 가장 평점이 높은 한우 레스토랑, 많은 미식가들이 꼽은 세계 최고의 한국 고깃집으로 가봅니다.

광고는 아니지만 너무 맛있는 음식을 해주신 민경환 셰프님과 Born & Bred 께 감사의 말씀을 전합니다.
