title: F1 Driver eats Bodyweight in Korean BBQ!! Ft. Alex Albon
description:
A few months ago we met the Thai British F1 driver Alex Albon at an event and invited him to go out and try some Korean food. Alex is one of only 20 Formula One drivers in the world, and is ranked amongst the very best drivers on the grid. He’s driven for Toro Rosso, Red Bull and most recently for Williams. During the mid-season break he took time out of his busy schedule to meet us at one of my favourite London BBQ spots for some food and a chat.

Thank you to Alex and his team for giving their time to film!
All the best for the Singapore Grand Prix!

Today's video is not sponsored, but let's support local businesses!
You can find : Chung'dam London, 35-36 Greek St, London W1D 5DL

--

몇달 전에 저희가 어떤 행사에서 태국계 영국 F1 레이싱 선수 알렉스 알본님을 만나뵈었는데요. 한국 음식을 소개해드리고 싶어서 언제 꼭 식사 대접하고 싶다고 말씀드렸죠. 참고로, 알렉스 님은 전 세계에서 20명에게만 주어지는 ' 포뮬러 원 드라이버' 라는 타이틀을 갖고 계실뿐만 아니라 그 중에서도 거의 탑급인 선수시죠. 토로 로쏘, 레드불 팀을 거쳐 현재는 윌리엄스 소속으로 뛰고 계신데요. 이번에 시즌 중에 잠깐 공백이 생긴 틈을 타, 특별히 저희를 위해 시간을 내주셔서 제가 가장 좋아하는 고깃집에 모시고 가서 식사하며 이야기를 나누게 되었답니다.

저희에게 시간과 촬영에 협조해주신 알렉스 선수님과 팀에게 감사의 말씀을 전합니다!

오늘 영상에 광고는 포함되어 있지 않지만, 지역 상인 여러분 늘 응원합니다!
주소: Chung'dam London, 35-36 Greek St, London W1D 5DL

title: British Students Eat Everything at a Korean Gas Stop!
description:
As we drove back to Seoul after an amazing few days in Jeollanam-do, we had a little rest at a Korean hyugeso (service station). This service station was on an island of its own and had the biggest array of food you can imagine! Also in this video, Armand and Max experience a bucket list 
moment when they find out they are sponsored by the drink they fell in love with since day one -  Milkis!

--

전라남도에서 잊지 못할 시간을 보내고 서울로 다시 올라오는 길에 그냥 지나칠 수 없는 코스가 있었죠. 바로, 아르망과 맥스에게 한국 휴게소 음식을 소개 시켜 주고 싶었어요! 그래서 섬 전체가 휴게소인 맛집 천지 행담휴게소로 갔는데 이렇게 까지 크고 다양한 음식이 있을 줄은 몰랐어요! 그리고 아르망과 맥스가 꿈에 그리던 일도 벌어졌는데요. 우리가 이 친구들을 처음 만났던 날 부터 사랑에 빠진 밀키스! 협찬을 받았습니다! 다이아 버튼 받는것 보다 더 좋아하는(?)  맥스의 반응... 영상으로 확인해주세요!

title: British Rappers try GIANT Korean King Crab!!!
description:
Today we take the British Rappers out to a Korean Seafood Market for the first time, for the freshest yellowtail, King Crab, Lobster, and just about every seafood you can imagine!!

This video wasn't sponsored but huge shout-out to 땅끝마을 (Ground floor, stall 109 and 110) and 하늘채별관 (restaurant) at Noryangjin Seafood Market for taking such good care of us, providing the freshest seafood and letting us film.

오늘은 영국래퍼를 데리고 신선한 해산물을 먹으러 노량진 수산시장에 왔어요! 방어, 킹크랩, 랍스터 그리고 다른 맛있는 해산물들까지 먹어볼수 있었어요.

이 영상은 협찬을 받지 않았지만, 저희에게 신선하고 해산물을 제공해주시고 친절하게 설명해주신 노량진 수산시장의 땅끝마을 (활어 109, 110호) 사장님과 하늘채별관 (5층 식당) 에 진심으로 감사드립니다!

title: K-POP Stars Introduce Their Favourite Food to Korean Englishman!!

title: David Beckham tries the BEST Korean BBQ Pork Belly for the first time!!
description:
Today, we got to treat David Beckham to Jeju-style black pork belly! There were so many foods we properly wanted to show Beckham, so we flew chefs from Korea to prepare a full course Korean meal! From Pork BBQ to Kimchi stew + steamed eggs + fried rice!!

Huge thanks to Sands China, the Macau Government Tourism Administration, and the staff of Samgeori Pugeutgan for helping prepare the food.

오늘은 베컴 형님께 제주식 흑돼지 삼겹살을 대접합니다! 소개해드리고 싶은 음식이 너무 많아서 아예 한국에서 셰프님들의 도움을 받아 풀코스로 준비했어요! 김치찌개 + 계란찜+ 볶음밥까지!! 과연 베컴 형님 입맛에 맞았을까요?! 🫢 

이번 영상 제작에 도움을 주신 샌즈 차이나, 마카오정부관광청에 감사의 말씀 전합니다. 
그리고 특별히 마카오 현지에서 음식 준비해주시느라 고생해주신 #삼거리푸줏간 관계자분들께 깊은 감사의 말씀 전합니다.

title: London Black-Cab Drivers try Korean Pigs Feet!!
description:
Today we’re back for a new video with some of our favourite guests: the London Cab drivers!
If you’re struggling, consider therapy with our paid partner. Click https://betterhelp.com/koreanenglishman for a discount on your first month of therapy.

Big thanks to the team at Gamnamuzip for bringing Korean Jokbal and Gamjatang to London! And for letting us film in the restaurant 🙏 

If you have questions about the brand relating to how the therapists are credentialed, their privacy policy, or therapist compensation, here is an overview written by the YouTube creators behind the channel Cinema Therapy that goes into these topics:https://www.reddit.com/r/cinema_therapy/comments/1dpriql/addressing_the_betterhelp_concerns_headon_deep/

혼자 끙끙 앓지마시고 BetterHelp과 상담을 받아보세요. https://betterhelp.com/koreanenglishman에서 첫 달 상담료를 할인받으실 수 있다고 해요.

오늘은 여러분이 많이 좋아해주셨던 영국 택시 기사님들을 다시 모시고 새로운 영상으로 돌아왔습니다!

title: 한국 치맥 제대로 처음 먹어보고 거의 울 뻔 한 영국 래퍼들!?
description:
Guvna B랑 Barney Artist 만날때 마다 항상 맛있는 한식을 먹으면서 즐거운 시간을 갖었죠. 이전 영상 찍고 나서 꼭 제대로 된 한국 치맥을 소개 시켜야 하겠다는 생각이 들었죠… 그치만 영국에서도 정말 맛있는 치킨집들이 많이 있지만 진정한 한국 치킨을 경험 할 수 있게 해주고 싶었어요!

감사하게도 현재 배달앱에서 1순위인 한국 치킨 브랜드한테 연학을 받자 영국에서 만들 수 있게 모든 재료들과 레시피를 보내줬답니다!

런던에서 치킨을 맛있게 만들어주고 열심히 수고해준 @gabiekook에게 빅 샤라웃 합니다!

그리고 모든 재료를 보내주시고 오늘 영상 후워 해주신 순살만 공격에게 감사의 말씀을 전합니다.

본 영상은 순살만공격 (http://순살만공격.com) 의 유료광고를 포함하고 있습니다.
#순살만공격 #치파포세트 #배민1위치킨 #ad

영상의 모든 출연진을 포함한 스태프 전원은 촬영 이전 코로나19 음성 확진을 받았습니다.
모든 촬영 과정은 정부의 방역 지침에 따라 전문가의 확인을 받아 진행되었습니다.

--

Ever since we met Guvna B and Barney Artist we’ve had so much fun with every Korean meal together. And after the last video we knew that we introduce them to proper Korean chicken and beer… but although there are some great options in the UK we wanted to give them a really authentic experience!

Thankfully the current number 1 delivery Chicken brand in Korea reached out to us and offered to send all of their ingredients and recipe to the UK so that we could make it here!

Huge shoutout to @gabiekook for doing such an incredible job recreating this Chicken here in London.

And of course big thanks to 순살만 공격 for sending us everything from Korea and for sponsoring this episode.

Everyone in this video including the crew had a negative COVID-19 test prior to shooting.
All activities were fully risk assessed and monitored in accordance with UK government guidelines.

#chimaek #rappers #ad

title: Ollie's Bold Korean Challenge 🤨
We rated every Fried Chicken in Korea.
description:
Today we try all the delivery chicken available and decide which chicken is the best Korean chicken! We started out by thinking we’d try about 5 franchise chicken places but ended up trying 10+ chickens in the end. Which one was our favourite? I guess you’ll have to watch the video to find out!

Thank you to The Kia EV9 for sponsoring this series!

--

한국에서 가장 맛있는 치킨 프랜차이즈는 어디일까요? 모두 각자 다 다른 브랜드를 생각하고 있지 않을까 싶은데요, 오늘은 이태리셰프 미스터 Z와 함께 영국남자피셜 베스트 배달치킨을 꼽아봅니다! 원래는 가장 유명한 프랜차이즈 5군데 정도를 비교하려고 했는데, 5군데가 10군데가 되고, 나중에는 몇가지를 먹어본지도 모르겠어요. 어쨌든 다 먹어보고 고른 저희의 PICK은? 영상에서 확인해주세요!

이 시리즈 제작에 도움을 주신 The Kia EV9께 감사의 말씀을 전합니다!

title: British Highschoolers go to a Korean Gaming Cafe for the first time!!
description:
Today the boys experience an epitomisation of Korean culture with high technology and good food at a Korean PC bang!

Today’s video is not sponsored, but thank you to Portal PC for allowing us to film!
Portal PC
Seoul, Seocho-gu, Seocho-Daero77-gil, 62 Basement floor 2 

--

오늘은 한국의 기술 + 음식 조합의 절정을 경험할 수 있는 한국 PC방을 영국 고딩들에게 소개 합니다!

오늘 영상에 광고는 포함되어 있지 않지만, 촬영을 할 수 있게 도와 주신 Portal PC께 감사의 말씀을 전합니다!
Portal PC
서울 서초구 서초대로77길 62 지하 2층

title: 미국의 가장 비싼 한국 고기집 $$$ (Feat. Buzzfeed 스티븐)
description:
LA에서의 마지막 밤에 버즈피드의 히트 시리즈 Worth It을 맡고 있는 스티븐 씨를 만났어요. 큰 인기를 얻었던 미국에 있는 한국 고깃집 영상에서 1위를 차지한 고깃집으로 저흴 데려가줬답니다. 

스티비 원더의 최애 식당에 가서 제 인생에서 가장 비싸고 맛있는 소고기를 먹어봤답니다! 

Check out Steven's latest Worth It series on Buzzfeed's YouTube channel: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9yh6-d_u-hA

For our last night in LA we met Steven from Buzzfeed's hit series Worth It, who had agreed to take us to the winner of their viral video about Korean BBQ in America. 

We get to go to one of Stevie Wonder's favourite restaurants and taste some of the most expensive, high quality beef I've ever tried!





Huge thanks to everyone involved in the creation of this video:
Written and Directed by Josh and Ollie
Producer/Editor: Grace Park
Producer/Editor/Translator: Hyemin Lim
Technical Director/Editor: Mike Kim
Editor/Translator: Sejun Lee

title: British Uni Students try Korean Chicken and Beer for the First Time!!
description:
Today we take Armand and Max for their final meal in Korea! We wanted to take them for Chimek since the first time they were in Korea but we were only able to do that now!
Thank you to everyone who watched and enjoyed this series! 

--

오늘은 아르망과 맥스의 한국에서의 마지막 만찬으로 치맥을 먹으러 갑니다! 처음 한국에 왔을때부터 소개 해주고 싶었던건데 마지막 식사를 하면서야 함께 먹을 수 있네요!
그동안 이 시리즈를 많이 사랑해주시고 시청해 주셔서 감사합니다!

title: Korean Navy Officers Try British Rations for the First Time!!
description:
In our last video we had the Korean navy officer introduce Korean navy rations to British Uni boys. And today we introduce British rations to Korean navy officers! It was an amazing cultural exchange and very interesting to see what Korean officers thought of the variety of courses in British army rations! What was their favourite part of the meal? Watch till the end to find out!:)

--

지난번 영상에서는 한국 군인분들이 영국친구들에게 한국 전투식량을 소개했는데요, 이번에는 영국 친구들이 한국 장병분들께 영국 전투식량을 소개합니다! 맛은 보장할 수 없지만 종류가 다양하기로 유명한 영국 전투식량! 과연 한국 장병분들의 최애메뉴는 뭐였을까요? 영상에서 확인해주세요!

title: 한국 술+안주를 처음 먹어본 데드풀의 반응!?
description:
#데드풀 #라이언레이놀즈 #ad
이번 인터뷰를 가능하게 해 주시고, 영상 제작에 후원해주신 20C FOX KOREA에 감사드립니다!
오늘은 라이언 레이놀즈 분장을 한 데드풀과 한강에서 소주 한 잔 해봅니다.

아니 근데 진짜, 라이언 레이놀즈씨가 전용 보트로 데드풀2 레드카펫 행사장에 가시는 동안 저희에게 동행할 수 있는 영광이 주어졌답니다. 그리고 제 친구 이모님께서 기꺼이 도와주신 덕분에 라이언씨께 제대로 된 한국 음식을 대접하고 어떻게 생각하시는지 여쭤볼 수 있었어요. 한국 술, 한국 안주, 한강, 그리고 데드풀의 실사판. 저희가 인터뷰하면서 이렇게 많이 웃은건 또 처음인 것 같아요.

오늘 영상의 진정한 주인공 박정숙 이모님께 감사드립니다!!


Today we did Soju shots with Deadpool wearing a Ryan Reynolds costume on the Han River. 

Seriously though, we had that privilege to join Ryan Reynolds on his private boat ride to the Deadpool 2 red carpet event in Seoul. And thanks to my friend's Aunt, got to see what he thinks of some seriously legit Korean food. Korean Alcohol, Korean Anju, the Han River and Deadpool incarnate. We haven't ever laughed this much in an interview. 


Written and Directed by Josh and Ollie
Camera work: Joel Bennett and Ollie
Producer/Editor: Grace Park
Producer/Editor/Translator: Hyemin Lim
Technical Director/Editor: Mike Kim
Editor/Translator: Sejun Lee

title: 삼겹살에 치즈김치볶음밥 처음 먹어본 영국 고등학생들의 반응!?
description:
오늘은 영국 고등학생들에게 삼겹살 바베큐 풀코스를 소개 했습니다!
작년에 저희가 직접 만든 고기불판 테이블에 먹었는데, 베이컨만 알고 살아온 영국친구들에게 고기 다 굽고 먹는 김치볶음밥이 통할까요?

영상의 모든 출연진을 포함한 스태프 전원은 촬영 이전 코로나19 음성 확진을 받았습니다.
모든 촬영 과정은 정부의 방역 지침에 따라 전문가의 확인을 받아 진행되었습니다.

--
Today we take British high school students on a full course Korean Pork Belly BBQ experience.
Starting from the meat, then the Ssam, and finishing with a classic Kimchi fried rice!

Everyone in this video including the crew had a negative COVID-19 test prior to shooting.
All activities were fully risk assessed and monitored in accordance with UK government guidelines.

title: British Uni Students Fly to Korea for the Best Korean BBQ!!
description:
Armand and Max are finally back in Korea, and this time as uni students! We have an amazing week planned out for them but first things first. First meal back in Korea: Pork BBQ with soju!

Huge thanks to Andaz Hotel for letting us film. #andaz

--

여러분 저희가 아르망과 맥스 대학교 방학 하자마자 한국 데려왔어요! 빨리 칭찬해주세요!! 공항 도착 하자마자 고기앞으로 데려가서 눈물의 삼겹살+소주 먹방 합니다!!

촬영에 도움을 주신 Andaz Hotel에 감사의 말씀드립니다. #안다즈 #andaz

title: Sonny introduces Korean BBQ to his Spurs team mates
description:
Son Heung-min introduces his Spurs team mate to the best Korean Beef BBQ

Check out the full video on the channel

https://youtu.be/TvFxQIvUHms

title: What I said to the Korean President
MLB Players Try Korean Baseball Stadium Food for the first time!!
description:
We’re back!! With a very special video today as the LA Dodgers and SD Padres came to Korea to open the season for the first time ever!
We had a chance to meet some of the players and show them some of our favourite iconic Korean Ballpark foods and snacks, in a Baseball Stadium here in Seoul!

We’ve missed you all! We hope you enjoy the video, and stay for tuned next week when we’ll be starting a very exciting new series 👀
#mlb #ladodgers #sdpadres 
--

저희가 돌아왔어요 여러분! 오늘은 특별한 영상을 준비해봤는데요, 얼마 전 사상 최초로 MBL 개막전을 한국에서 열었죠, 그래서 저희가 LA 다저스와 SD파드레스 선수들을 만나고 왔습니다!
선수분들께 대표적인 한국 야구장 음식들을 소개해드렸답니다!

여러분 너무너무 보고싶었어요!! 이번 영상도 재밌게 봐주셨음 좋겠고, 다음 주 부터 정말 야심찬 시리즈가 시작될 예정이니 채널고정 부탁드려요!! 👀

title: British Highschoolers try School Lunch in Korea!!
description:
While visiting Korea and having the time of their lives, we also wanted to give the boys an experience of what the lives of High Schoolers their age in Korea are like.

After arriving at the school and joining a class with their Korean classmates, it was time for lunch and some dodgeball! Next episode on Wednesday so stay tuned!

--

이번에 한국에 와서 우리 영국 고딩들이 할 수 있는 여러 체험 중에 한국 학생으로서의 하루를 꼭 경험하게 해주고 싶었어요.

한국 학교에서의 첫 수업을 마치고 난 뒤, 급식을 먹고 피구를 했어요! 다음 영상은 수요일에 올라가니 모두 많이 기대해주세요!

저희에게 시간을 내주시고 촬영에 흔쾌히 응해주신 염광고등학교에 감사합니다.

title: 처음 김밥을 먹어본 어벤져스 배우들의 반응?! (실화)
description:
#ad #어벤져스 #스파이더맨 #로키 #닥터스트레인지 #맨티스 #톰홀랜드 #베네딕트컴버배치 #톰히들스턴 #폼클레멘티에프
이번 인터뷰를 가능하게 해 주시고, 영상 제작에 후원해주신 월트디즈니 컴퍼니 코리아에 감사드립니다!
이번 주에는 영화 ‘어벤져스: 인피니티 워’ 배우 네 분이랑 촬영할 수 있는 기회가 주어졌어요!! 호텔에서 엄청 바쁜 일정을 소화하고 계시던 배우분들께 한국에서 재밌고 독특한 추억을 만들어드리고 싶어서, 저희가 호텔 안에 직접 만든 특별한 세트장으로 배우분들을 모셨습니다!!

배우분들의 식단에 맞춰 어떤 음식을 소개할까 고민하던 중에, 그동안 저희가 외국인분들에게 소개해드렸던 음식 중 인기도 많고 배우분들의 식습관과 맞는 한국의 음식들을  준비했어요. 저는 어렸을 때 한국 친구들이 엄마가 싸 주시는 김밥을 도시락으로 가져와서 처음 먹어보게 됐는데요! 서울에서 제가 가장 좋아하는 두 김밥집의 속이 가득찬 여러 종류의 맛있는 김밥들과 영국에서는 볼 수 없는 신선하고 맛있는 한국의 과일들을 어벤져스 배우분들께 소개해 드렸답니다!!

한국음식을 먹어보는 어벤져스 4인방!

한국의 음식을 맛있게 드셔주시고, 친절하게 촬영에 참여해주신 네 배우분들께 진심을 다해 감사의 말씀을 전합니다.

Benedict Cumberbatch tries Kimbap for the first time, Pom Klementieff is mind blown by Korean pears, Tom Holland falls in love with Korean Banana Milk and Tom Hiddleston may have the smoothest voice in history. This week we have the privilege of filming with 4 members of the cast of Avengers Infinity War!!

With a busy schedule of press interviews in their hotel, we wanted to give them all a unique, fun Korean experience during their stay. So we put together a set and chose a variety of homely Korean food that we know foreigners love from our previous videos, that also fit the actors' dietary requirements. Kimbap from two of my favourite Kimbap joints in Seoul, the sort of Kimbap bursting with fillings and flavour, that makes me question how on earth Kimbap isn't known around the world. And fresh juicy Korean fruit that is unlike anything we have in the UK. 

This is Avengers trying Korean food!


Huge thanks to everyone involved in the creation of this video:
Written and Directed by Josh and Ollie
Producer/Editor: Grace Park
Producer/Editor/Translator: Hyemin Lim
Technical Director/Editor: Mike Kim
Editor/Translator: Sejun Lee

title: 한국 치맥+떡볶이 콤보에 반해버린 스파이더맨 베프 네드!!?
description:
#스파이더맨 #치킨 #Ad
이번 인터뷰를 가능하게 해 주시고, 영상 제작에 후원해주신 Sony Korea와 60계 치킨에 감사드립니다!
오늘은 쌍둥이 시리즈 중간에 특별한 토요일 에피소드를 준비했어요. 한국에서 스파이더맨의 베프, 제이콥 배덜런씨와 촬영할 기회가 있었거든요!! 지난번에 태런 에저튼씨를 다시 만났을 때 왜 치맥은 없냐며 실망하셨던 사건 이후로 제이콥 배덜런씨에게는 꼭 제대로된 치맥 경험을 하게 해드려야겠다고 생각했어요. 이번엔 떡볶이랑 엄청 매운 치킨도 한번 먹어보고요!!

저희 채널에서 만났던 게스트 중에 가장 겸손하셨던 제이콥씨, 촬영 내내 정말 즐거웠답니다!

이번 인터뷰를 준비해주시고 스폰서해주신 소니코리아에 큰 감사의 말씀 드립니다. 이번 촬영장소였던 60계 치킨에도 감사의 말씀 전합니다. 치킨 완전 강추강추!!

이제 '스파이더맨: 파 프롬 홈'을 유튜브와 구글플레이에서 볼수있어요!!
· 유튜브: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zsehQ83TtjI
· 구글플레이: https://play.google.com/store/movies/details/?id=zsehQ83TtjI


Today we’re uploading a Saturday mid-twin-series special, cos we got the chance to film with Spider-Man’s Best Friend, Jacob Batalon in Korea!! After our last meeting with Taron Egerton, who was clearly disappointed that we didn’t give him Korean Fried Chicken again, we thought we’d try to make up for it with Jacob Batalon, and give him a complete Chimek experience, with ddukbokki on the side and some seriously spicy fire chicken as well! 

One of the most down to earth guests we’ve ever had on the channel, Jacob was such a pleasure to film with! 

Big thanks to Sony Korea for setting this interview up and sponsoring the episode. And thank you to 60계 치킨, the chicken place we took him too. If you’re in Korea and want some good chicken then I couldn’t recommend this place more.

title: Basic Korean 01 : Greeting, "I," "You," 반말(informal) vs 존댓말 (Formal)
description:
Hey guys! So I started wearing braces... as you might have noticed midway from the slurpiness here and there. It's hard for me to speak and pronounce some words correctly, both Korean and English. But, I tried my best.. sorta. Thank goodness I recorded at least some bits before having braces inside my mouth. If you have any questions or suggestion, leave them in the comments! I look forward to reading them. 
••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••­­­•••••••••••
Connect with me here:
INSTAGRAM: http://instagram.com/explorekorean
WEBSITE : http://explorekorean.net
••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••­­­•••••••••••
Review Sheet

Dialogue #1
Jiyeon: 안녕하세요 (Hello)
Jaeho: 안녕하세요 (Hello)
Jiyeon: 저는 이지연이에요. (I am Jiyeon Lee)
Jaeho: 저는 김재호입니다. 만나서 반갑습니다. (I am Jaeho Kim. Nice to meet you.)
Jiyeon: 네, 저도 만나서 반가워요 (Yes it's nice to meet you too.)

Dialogue #2
Jisoo: 안녕 (Hello)
Seoyoung: 안녕 (Hello)
Jisoo: 너는 이름이 뭐야? (What is your name?)
Seoyoung: 난 박서영이야. 넌? (I am Seoyoung Park. And you are?)
Jisoo: 난 이지수야. 너 정말 예쁘다 (I am Jisoo Lee. You are really pretty.)
Seoyoung: 고마워 (Thank you.)

Dialogue #3
Jisoo: 선생님 안녕하세요 (Hello, teacher.)
Teacher: 응 지수도 안녕. 잘 지내니? (Yes, hello to Jisoo too. Are you doing well?) 
Jisoo: 네, 선생님은요? (Yes, how about you?)

*Expressions

★Formal 존댓말
I am ___ (noun)
저는 ___ 입니다.
저는 ___ 이에요 (when the word ends with 받침)
저는 ___ 예요 (when the word ends without 받침)

저는 사람입니다. I'm a person.
저는 여자예요. I'm a woman.
저는 선생님이에요. I'm a teacher.

만나서 반갑습니다. 
만나서 반가워요
Nice to meet you!

★Casual 반말
I am ___ (noun)
나는 ___ 이야 (when the word ends with 받침)
나는 ___ 야  (when the word ends without 받침)

나는 사람이야. (I'm a person.)
나는 빅키야. (I'm Vicky)

너는 이름이 뭐야? (What is your name?)

나 I
너 you
이름 name
예쁘다 pretty

What's the word for "you" in formal form?
-Please watch the video to find out! 
(if you missed it, it starts from 06:00 in this vid)

#Korean #LearnKorean #KoreanLesson

title: 10+ Phrases for Beginners to Start Korean With
description:
[Summary Note]
1. Hello!
안녕하세요 (f)
안녕 (if)

2. Bye!
안녕히 가세요 (f) - used when the other person is leaving
안녕히 계세요  (f) - used when you are leaving 
안녕 (if)

3. Yes 
네 (f)
응 (if)

No
아니요(f)
아니(if)

4.Ok. (Got it)
알겠어요 (f)
알았어요 (f)

알겠어 (if)
알았어 (if)

5. Thank you.
감사합니다.(f)
고맙습니다.(f)
감사해요.(f)
고마워요.(f)

고마워(if)

6. Sorry.
죄송합니다 (f)
죄송해요 (f)
미안해요 (f)

미안해 (f)

7. Excuse me.
실례합니다 (but not used often, if not at all)

Alternative 1. 
잠시만요. (f)
지나갈게요 (f)

Alternative 2. 
저기요 (f)

I didn't put the informal form of excuse me, 
because the word "excuse me" already has a formal meaning, so the informal form isn't used often.

8. I am from _______.
저는 ______ 사람이예요. (f)

저는 미국 사람이예요.
저는 필리핀 사람이예요.
저는 인도 사람이예요.

(I didn't put it in the video but you can also say 사람입니다 instead of 사람이예요.)
저는 _______ 사람입니다. (also f)

나는 _______ 사람이야. (if)
나는 중국 사람이야.
나는 한국 사람이야

9. I can't speak Korean.
저 한국어 잘 못해요. (f)
저 한국어 잘 몰라요. (f)

나 한국어 잘 못해 (if)
나 한국어 잘 몰라 (if)

10. My name is _________.
제 이름은 _______ 예요. (f)
(제 이름은 ________ 입니다 is also another formal form I didn't mention.)

제 이름은 제프예요.(f)
제 이름은 레이첼이예요. (f)

내 이름은 _______야. (if)
내 이름은 빅키야. (if)
내 이름은 심효정이야. (if)

Watch the whole video for a detailed explanation. 화이팅!

title: All about "이다" verb (이에요, 예요 / 이었어요, 였어요 / 일 거예요)

01:41 Using 이다 in a sentence (Present Tense)
08:43 examples where you shouldn't use 이다

title: How to say “I’m __ Years Old” in Korean
description:
Lesson on "how to say how old you are, and how to ask how old another person is in Korean" 

나이 (age) – Native Korean Number
저는 ___ 살이에요. (존댓말)
나는 ___ 살이야. (반말)

하나, 둘, 셋 넷 + MW = 한, 두, 세, 네

Birth year – Sino Korean Number
저는 ____ 년생이에요.

How old are you?
나이가 어떻게 되세요?
연세가 어떻게 되세요?
몇 살 이세요?
몇 살 이야?

title: Question Word 몇 & Counter Words (-개, -명, -마리, -시, -분, -달 and more)
description:
In this lesson, we learn how to use 몇 (how many) and counter words that can follow it. 몇 is used when you are asking for specific number of things, people or animals, and it can also be used when asking someone's age, what time it is, or how many hours/months it takes.

#몇 #KoreanCounterWord

title: How to Say "Because" in Korean
description:
This lesson might be a bit difficult to follow, so I tried to include many example sentences so that you can put into practice what we learned right away. Also, ‘ Verb + 서’ expression is used more often (in spoken Korean) than Verb + (이)기 때문에, so please don’t worry too much if you find verb conjugation with 때문에 a bit hard. 때문에 is more used in “Noun + 때문에 (blaming Noun)” expression, which I cover at the very last part of this lesson, so if you understand that one easily, it’s already a good progress :) 파이팅!!!

+) I forgot to include that you can also put “왜냐하면” before “-때문이에요” phrase. So when you are answering to the question “왜 벌써 퇴근해요?(Why get off work already?)” you can answer “몸이 안좋기 때문이에요” like we learned but also “왜냐하면 몸이 안좋기 때문이에요” works too. But you can’t say “왜냐하면 몸이 안좋기 때문에 일찍 퇴근해요(x)” because by putting 왜냐하면, you are already making it a full sentence. 왜냐하면 is pronounced (wae-nya-ha-myun). You can also just say 왜냐면 (wae-nya-myun) without 하. 

+) there’s also "verb+ -(으)니까" expression which means the same as verb+ –서, and we’ll cover that later

title: Sentence Making Practice #2 (Friday Review Class)
description:
Live Class Streamed on July 31st 1pm (KST)

Hi guys! First of all, thank you so much for the heartfelt, supportive, and encouraging comments and private messages. I haven't had a chance to reply to all of them, but it made me feel so relieved and much better. I was honestly quite scared, but it feels good to know that a lot of you guys are so kind to understand. Thank you also for the heartfelt advice. I will take them to heart.

*Special thanks to @Robyn for helping me out as a moderator in today's class ♥ 
-------------------------------------------------------------
🌸Time Stamp🌸
02:30 First Homework (-는 것 & -기)
41:06 Second Homework #1 (-고 생각해요)
01:06:45 Second Homework #2 (-것 같아요)
---------------------------------------------------------------
This is a review class we had based on two homework I gave out to you guys :) 

First Homework: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1gx3mfllPEHiITGxPI_lKfVV_jPZqFNYQ/view?usp=sharing

Second Homework: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1L1X29BtGgLomuI7QWeafz8RlizXur_sw/view?usp=sharing

Please make sure you finished the homework before joining. If you haven't finished yet, or are not familiar yet with making sentences with -기, -것, -것 같아요, -라고 생각해요, then this class may be difficult for you to catch up, so it is recommended that you first finish two previous classes (saved live class) on my channel. It's also okay to learn how the above expressions/grammar work form other sources. The class will be saved afterwards, so no need to worry if you can't join.

title: TOPIK 1 Listening Class (Live)
description:
Live Class Streamed on Sept 19th, 1pm (KST)

TOPIK 1 Test Sheet & Instruction
https://explorekorean.com/class-schedule/

If you haven't heard of TOPIK, check out this post:
https://explorekorean.com/2020/09/17/what-is-a-topik-test/

This class is for the listening section of the test, and Sept 20th class will be for the reading section. 

It is recommended that you finish the test and then join. We might not get to cover all 70 questions in class, but I’m aiming to do as many as possible. We'll see. Before class, you can request for the questions in the test that you’d like me to cover. Leave them here in live chat, and I’ll make sure to include the most requested ones.

title: Let's Play "Would You Rather" in Korean (밸런스 게임)
description:
[Class Notes(used in this class)]
Would You Rather Questions & Vocabs & Grammar Note : https://explorekorean.net/extra-class-notes/?vid=5

밸런스 게임(balance game) is a popular game in Korea, and basically similar to answering “Would you rather” questions. You are given two choices (of hypothetical situations), and you can only choose one of them. Each choice has its own benefits and drawbacks that are of perfect “balance” (thus the “balance” game), and that makes it a lot more difficult for you to make a final choice, which is supposed to be the whole point of this game haha. Some choices only have drawbacks, and it’s up to you to decide which one is better / less worse than the other! ㅎㅎ 

Let's play this game together and learn fun new Korean words, phrases and grammar at the same time. Before we start the game, we'll go over some of the grammar used in the questions/choices :) 
••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••­­­•••••••••••

[Live Class Schedule]
Korean Passive Verbs #2 - Vocab Class (-이, -리) - May 8th, 11am KST

Korean Passive Verbs #3 - Vocab Class (-히, -기, -아/어지다) - May 17th, (Time : TBA)

Korean Passive Verbs #3 - Vocab Class (-되다, -받다, -당하다) - May 26th, (Time : TBA)

In our upcoming 3 live classes on Passive Verbs, we'll focus on memorizing the vocabs (with visual aids like images or videos) and also making sentences. title: Why does 뭐 (MWO) sound like 붜 (BWO)? | m, ㅁ and ㅂ
description:
Is it 뭐 or 붜? 무슨 or 부슨?
This is one question I get asked a lot from many learners. Hope this video helps you distinguish m, ㅁ and ㅂ sound!

title: Korean Alphabet #1 - Start Your Korean Here! (Pronunciation, Vocab & Listening Practice)
description:
This lesson is perfect for complete beginners, or anyone looking to brush up on their 한글 skills! 

We'll start by learning the basic Korean consonants and vowels. Along the way, you'll learn how to pronounce each letter correctly and even practice some simple Korean words.

We'll also have fun activities, including reading practice, listening (dictation) exercises, and sound recognition games to train your ears and help you become more familiar with 한글.

So grab a pen, get comfortable, and let's take our very first step into the world of 한글! 🇰🇷✨📚

*한글 (Hangeul) - The Korean Alphabet 🌼

title: [Vocabulary Class] Hard-to-Translate Emotion Words in Korean
description:
Streamed July 19th, 9 pm (Korea Standard Time) 

02:10 Intro & Conjunction -서
05:48 One thing to note before we start
07:57 답답하다
14:01 민망하다
19:55 뻘쭘하다
24:28 섭섭하다
31:10 서운하다
34:55 아쉽다
39:13 시원하다
48:51 시원섭섭하다
52:10 속상하다
56:33 삐지다
01:02:53 서럽다
01:09:30 힘들다
01:15:54 Outro, Review Note & Thank you :)

We learn 12 emotion words in Korean, (답답하다, 민망하다, 뻘쭘하다, 섭섭하다, 서운하다, 아쉽다, 시원하다 / 후련하다, 시원섭섭하다, 속상하다, 삐지다, 서럽다, 힘들다) that are a bit hard to translate (or don't have the exact equivalent word) in English, and how they are used. We also learn many other commonly used vocabularies as we practice making sentences with them.

You can find the review note here:
https://drive.google.com/file/d/1gftxIhsMKU-CGXrekpMgxCoVRMKzpgLf/view?usp=sharing

It's the exact same file I used in this class :) 

Thank you again for joining~ 
I'll see you in the next lesson!

channel_title: Learn Korean with GO! Billy Korean
title: Billy Go’s Beginner Korean Course | #2: Intro to 한글
description:
Welcome back! This lesson will introduce the Korean alphabet known as Hangul (한글). I’ll also talk about some of the downsides to using Romanization, and stroke order. Then you’ll learn our first few letters, and make your first syllables.

Want a textbook to start learning Korean? Check out my book, "Korean Made Simple" on Amazon: http://amzn.to/2bDBi6h (affiliate)

Here’s the full playlist for this course: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLbFrQnW0BNMUkAFj4MjYauXBPtO3I9O_k

title: Billy Go’s Beginner Korean Course | #6: Learning 한글 Part 4 More Syllables
description:
This lesson introduces the last two syllable blocks, explains how to read multiple syllables together using the consonant ㅇ, and teaches how to deal with syllables that have two consonants on the bottom.

channel_title: Learn Korean with GO! Billy Korean
title: Billy Go’s Beginner Korean Course | #8: Learning 한글 Part 6 Diphthongs
description:
This lesson introduces seven diphthongs (two vowels that are read as one), which are the final new “letters” to learn, completing all of the letters that we need to learn for the Korean alphabet 한글.
Want a textbook to start learning Korean? Check out my book, "Korean Made Simple" on Amazon: http://amzn.to/2bDBi6h (affiliate)

title: Billy Go’s Beginner Korean Course | #41: Intro to Politeness Levels
description:
In this lesson I’ll introduce you to politeness levels, and explain more about how to use the (으)세요 form, as well as the (으)시다 form. You’ll also review how to use Honorific Speech, and when not to use it. And finally, I’ll explain some exceptions you’ll need to know when conjugating the (으)세요 form.

description:
In this lesson I’ll show you how you can quickly change the regular Informal conjugation we’ve been using into the Past Tense. I’ll also talk about how the Past Tense works differently than the Present Tense we’ve been using up until this point. And you’ll learn how to use the Past Tense together with the Honorific Ending (으)시다.

description:
In this lesson I’ll teach you how to say various question words, such as who, what, where, when, why, and how. I’ll also explain which words are nouns and which are adverbs, as well as talk about a few different ways that some of them are used. description:
In this lesson I’ll teach you how to use the particle 에서. I’ll also compare and contrast it with the particle 에, and explain how 에서 can be used in two different situations. You’ll also learn a common exception with 에 and 에서 with the verb 살다 (‘to live’).

description:
In this lesson I’ll teach you about the most common negative verbs, as well as how to make your own negative verbs using two different forms. You’ll also learn when and where to use each of those two forms. And you’ll learn how to use “Yes” and “No” correctly when answering a negative question. Finally, you’ll also learn about negative adverbs.

description:
In this lesson you’ll learn two ways to say ‘to’ and ‘from’ a person. You’ll also learn how to say ‘to’ and ‘from’ a location. And you’ll learn a pronunciation tip for the consonant ㅎ. Finally, I’ll explain why you should use ‘to’ or ‘from’ when talking about words related to communicating.

title: Billy Go’s Beginner Korean Course | #49: Humble Speech
description:
In this lesson you’ll learn how and when to use honorific speech and humble speech. You’ll also learn more about honorific verbs, humble verbs, honorific nouns, honorific particles, as well as humble nouns. And finally, I’ll explain some of the major factors that native speakers use to decide what level of politeness to use when speaking with another person.

description:
In this lesson you’ll learn how to use a particle we previously learned – 와 and 과 – to mean ‘with.’ You’ll also learn two other ways to say ‘with,’ including the particles 하고, and 이랑 or 랑. And you’ll learn about 같이, a common adverb that’s used together with these particles.

description:
In this lesson you’ll learn how to say let’s do something, as well as how to make suggestions by saying ‘shall we’ or ‘shall I’ do something. You’ll also learn how to do this with honorific speech. And you’ll learn one more commonly used form to make suggestions which is not recommended.

In this lesson you’ll learn how to use the verb 있다, meaning ‘to exist,’ to also mean ‘to have.’ You’ll also learn how to use the verb 가지다 (or 갖다) to mean ‘to have,’ together with the progressive tense. And you’ll learn how to avoid a common mistake when using 있다 to mean ‘to have.’

description:
In this lesson you’ll learn how to ask someone if they’re doing well. You’ll also learn how to ask how they’re doing, as well as how to reply if someone asks the same question to you. And you’ll learn how to say that it’s been a long time since you last saw someone.

channel_title: Learn Korean with GO! Billy Korean
title: Billy Go’s Beginner Korean Course | #55: Comparisons
description:
In this lesson you’ll learn how to compare nouns to other nouns. You’ll learn the most basic way to make comparisons, by saying something is “more” or “less” than something else. You’ll also learn how to say that a noun is the same as, different than, or similar to another noun. And you’ll learn how to say that something is “like” a noun using both descriptive verbs and action verbs. Finally, you’ll learn how to say that a noun is the “most” or “best.”

Want a textbook to start learning Korean? Check out my book, "Korean Made Simple" on Amazon: http://amzn.to/2bDBi6h (affiliate)

description:
In this lesson you’ll learn how to ask for clarification in a conversation. You’ll learn how to ask simple follow-up questions, as well as how to ask the other person to say something again, and how to ask someone to speak more slowly. And you’ll learn how to say that you understand, or didn’t understand something.

You’ll also learn how to ask “what” or “what kind” of a noun. And you’ll learn a special trick that helps make it easier to use colors while you’re first learning them.

description:
In this lesson you’ll be introduced to the two Korean number systems – Sino Korean numbers and Pure Korean numbers. You’ll learn first about Sino Korean numbers, and how to count from 1 up to the billions. Also you’ll learn about counters. And you’ll learn how to count money, as well as several other common measurements.

In this lesson you’ll learn about Pure Korean numbers, and how to count from 1 up to 99. You’ll also learn how to say your age, and the age of others – including those who are older than you. And you’ll be shown a much more detailed self-introduction using several new concepts that we’ve learned.

channel_title: Learn Korean with GO! Billy Korean
title: Billy Go’s Beginner Korean Course | #63: Counting Everything
description:
In this lesson you’ll learn about how to use several of the most common counter nouns. For example, you’ll learn how to count people (as well as when using Honorific Speech), animals, and general things. You’ll learn how to ask “how many” with nouns. You’ll also learn how to count flat items, books, bottles, long and thin items, machines, and several other things. And you’ll learn how to use the plural Particle 들, and the Object Marker together with numbers and counters.

In this lesson you’ll learn how to tell the time and the date, including days, months, and years. And you’ll learn how to ask what time it is, and what day it is. title: Billy Go’s Beginner Korean Course | #70: Giving a Reason Part 1
description:
In this lesson you’ll learn about two essential grammar forms that can be used to mean “because.” These are the 서 form and the (으)니까 form. You’ll learn how to use each one, any exceptions for them, and most importantly how they’re different. You’ll learn casual ways to refer to yourself and your friends, as well as how to say “you.” And you’ll learn how to say “yes” and “no” when using casual speech, along with how to use (or not use) the object marker. You’ll also learn casual ways to say “Let’s,” and how to make commands.

In this lesson you’ll learn some phrases that you can use when going shopping. You’ll learn how to ask the price of items, how to exchange or return items, and how to ask for a plastic bag. You’ll also learn how to express what you’re looking for, and how to ask for things.

channel_title: Learn Korean with GO! Billy Korean
title: Billy Go’s Beginner Korean Course | #100: Finding Your Way
description:
In this lesson you’ll learn how to ask someone for directions to somewhere. You’ll also learn several words and phrases that you’ll need in order to understand what someone might reply to this sort of question. And you’ll learn some useful phrases that you can use when you’re confused or don’t understand something in Korean. Finally, we’ll summarize what we learned in this Beginner Korean Course, and talk about what you can do after finishing this series.

Want a textbook to start learning Korean? Check out my book, "Korean Made Simple" on Amazon: http://amzn.to/2bDBi6h (affiliate)

title: [ Elementary Korean 9 ]  는 / 은  (Topic Particle) , 입니다 / 입니까?
description:
At today's lesson, you will learn "는 / 은" and "입니다 / 입니까?"
By the end of this lesson, you will be able to introduce yourself in Korean. That’s so awesome!

🔷Homework
1. Creating a short self-introduction paragraph

Ex 1) 안녕하세요. 제 이름은 토미입니다. (Enter your name)
Hello, my name is Tammy.

Ex 2) 저는 한국어 강사입니다. (Enter your occupation)
I am a Korean teacher.

You can change the name and occupation parts from this template to complete your original self-introduction.

2. Memorizing Vocabulary
40 words on the first page of my 1600-word for beginners.

title: [ Elementary Korean 10 ]  이/가 아닙니다  (Negative Sentence),  이것, 그것, 저것, 무엇
description:
Last class, we learned how to end a sentence using 입니다 which is [is, are, am] in English. In opposite, today, we will make a negative sentence using [아닙니다], which is [is not~].
Also, by the end of today’s class, we will learn how to use [이것, 그것, 저것, 무엇] [this,  that, that, what] to make sentences like [What is this?] “It is water” and etc.

Grammar Quiz (5-10 Lessons)👇
https://learning-korean.com/elementary/20210315-10777/

🔷Homework
1. Creating a short self-introduction paragraph

Ex ) 이것은 물이 아닙니다. 이것은 술입니다.
This is not water. This is an alcohol

title: [ Elementary Korean 11 ]  ㅂ/습니다  (present tense of formal style) , ~(하)고
description:
Today’s topic is [남자 친구 있습니까?] "Do you have a boyfriend?"
we will learn the grammar, [ㅂ/습니다], the polite form of present sentence ending, and [~(하)고] which means "(do (A) and (B)".

🔷Homework
1. Creating a short self-introduction paragraph

Ex) 저는 아침에 요가하고 밥 먹고 회사에 갑니다. 
      (Tell me your today’s schedule by connecting three verbs.)
       I do yoga in the morning, and eat rice and go to a company.

title: [ Elementary Korean 12 ]  ～지 않습니다 ,  안～  (Negative Expressions)
description:
In the previous class, we learned the expression [이것은 필통이 아닙니다] “This is not a pencil case.” This was a negative sentence for a noun. 
Today, we will learn how to make negative sentences for verbs and adjectives. 

🔷Homework
1. Creating a short self-introduction paragraph

Ex 1) 저는 담배 피우지 않습니다.
　　  I don’t smoke.

Ex 2) 저는 술 안 마십니다.
          I don’t drink alcohol. 

2. Memorizing Vocabulary
40 words on the second page of my 1600-word for beginners.

title: [ Elementary Korean 13 ]  예요/이에요 , 이/가 아니에요  (Negative Expressions)
description:
The theme of today’s class is [이 사진은 무슨 사진이에요?] “What photo is this?”
The grammar we will learn is [예요/이에요(It is~) and 、이/가 아니에요(It is not~)]. 

🔷Homework
1. Let's make a sentence using the grammar of [이에요/예요 (is)] and [이/가 아니에요 (is not)].

Ex) 제 출신은 동경이에요. 서울이 아니에요.
My hometown is Tokyo. I am not from Seoul.

title: [ Elementary Korean 15 ]  을/를  ,  -네요  , Sino-Korean Numbers
description:
The theme of today’s class is [생일이 언제예요?] “When is your birthday?” 
The grammar we will learn is the object marker [을/를], how to count numbers, and the expression [네요] “~isn’t it”. 

Grammar Quiz (11-15 Lessons)👇
https://learning-korean.com/elementary/20210419-10871/

🔷Homework
1. Let's write Korean sentences
A. Write your birthday in Hangul instead of numbers. 
Ex) 제 생일은 이천 사년 팔월 칠일입니다. 
       My birthday is August 7, 2004.

B. Let's use the expression [을 / 를] to describe what you usually do on your birthday. 
Ex) 생일 때는 미역국을 먹어요. 
       I eat seaweed soup on my birthday.

2. Memorizing Vocabulary
40 words on the first page of my 1600-word for beginners.

title: [ Elementary Korean 16 ]  과/와 ,  Native Korean Numbers ,  Interrogatives'
description:
The theme of today’s class is [저와 같이 영화 봐요.] “Let’s watch a movie together.” 
The grammar we will learn today is [과/와] “and”, and native Korean numbers and interrogatives.

🔷Homework
1. Let's write Korean sentences

① Let's write the current time in Korean.
Ex) 지금은 열두 시 삼 분입니다.
It's 12：03 now.

② Use [과 / 와] to tell me your favorite Korean food, dramas or pop songs
Ex) 저는 떡볶이와 김밥을 좋아합니다.
I like Tteokbokki and Kimbab.

title: [ Elementary Korean 17 ]  ~고 있어요  (present progressive tense) , (이)랑
description:
The title of today’s class is 【친구들이랑 춤을 배우고 있어요.】 “I am learning dance with my friends”. 
In today’s class, we will learn the present progressive tense [-하고 있어요.], and [(이)랑] “with or and.”

🔷Homework
1. Let's write Korean sentences.

 Let's use the present progressive tense [고 있어요] (ing) and [(이) 랑] “with/and” to express who is doing what. 
Ex) 저는 학생들이랑 한국 드라마를 보고 있어요. 
       I am watching Korean drama with my students.

title: [ Elementary Korean 18 ]  이었어요/였어요 ,  이요/요  (past & present tense ending for nouns)
description:
Today’s theme is [무슨 시험이었어요?] “What test was it?”
We will learn about the past tense ending for nouns [이었어요/였어요] “was/were,” and the present tense ending for nouns [이요/요] “is/am/are.” 

🔷Homework
1. Let's write Korean sentences

① Let's use the past tense for nouns 이었어요 / 였어요 was/were.
Ex) 어제는 친구 생일이었어요.
       Yesterday was my friend's birthday.

② Let's make a past tense negative for nouns. 
Ex) 모레가 아니었어요.
      It wasn't the day after tomorrow.

2. Memorizing Vocabulary
40 words on the first page of my 1600-word for beginners.
Vocabulary list➡https://learning-korean.com/elementary/20210101-10466/

description:

title: [ Elementary Korean 20 ]  (으)ㄹ 거예요 , (으)ㄹ게요 (future tense practice & examples)
description:
The theme of today’s class is 【가을 단풍이 참 아름다울 거예요.】 ”Autumn leaves will be very beautiful.” 
In today’s class, you will learn the future tense, such as [(으)ㄹ 거예요.] “It will be~” and [(으)ㄹ게요.] “I will~”. 

Grammar Quiz (16-20 Lessons)👇
https://learning-korean.com/elementary/20210524-11024/

🔷Homework
1. Let's write Korean sentences

① Please, use [(으)ㄹ 거예요] to write your schedule for tomorrow. 
Ex) 내일 제 친구가 미국에서 올 거예요.
       My friend will come from the United States tomorrow.

② Please use [(으)ㄹ게요] to tell me your determination and promises in learning Korean in the future.
Ex) 앞으로도 매주 숙제를 올릴게요.
       I will continue to do my homework every week.

2. Memorizing Vocabulary
40 words on the first page of my 1600-word for beginners.
Vocabulary list➡https://learning-korean.com/elementary/20210101-10466/

title: [ Elementary Korean 21 ]  (으)ㄹ까요? , (으)ㄹ까 봐  , (으)ㄹ까 하다 (future tense)
description:
The theme of today’s class is 【몇 시에 만날까요?】 “What time should we meet?”
In today’s class, we are going to learn trio of [(으)ㄹ까] , which are [(으)ㄹ까요?], [(으)ㄹ까 봐] and [(으)ㄹ까 하다]. 

🔷Homework
1. Let's write Korean sentences

① Let’s use [(으)ㄹ까 봐] to express that  you are worried about something. 
Ex) 현금이 모자랄까 봐 걱정이에요.
       I'm worried about running out of cash.

② Let's use [(으) ㄹ 까 하다] to express how you are going to solve what you are worried about in the previous sentence.
Ex) 그래서 오전에 은행에 갈까 해요.
       So I think I'll go to the bank in the morning.

2. Memorizing Vocabulary
40 words on the first page of my 1600-word for beginners.
Vocabulary list➡https://learning-korean.com/elementary/20210101-10466/
Challenge the 40-Word Quiz➡https://learning-korean.com/elementary/20210531-11041/#toc11

title: [ Elementary Korean 22 ]   (으)ㄹ래요 (shall we) , 지요"(isn't it?)
description:
The theme of today’s class is 【슬슬 전철역에 갈래요?】”Shall we go to the train station soon?” 
In today’s class, we’ll learn how to use [(으)ㄹ래요] “Shall we/be going to/will”  and [지요] “isn’t it?/ right?”. 

🔷Homework
1. Let's write Korean sentences

① Please tell me what you will do after you finish watching this video. 
Ex) 저는 카푸치노 마시고 조금 쉴래요.
       I will have a cappuccino and rest for a while.

② Please ask me if there is anything you want to confirm with me.
Ex) 토미 선생님, 한국 음식 좋아하죠?
       Tammy, you like Korean food, right?

2. Memorizing Vocabulary
40 words on the first page of my 1600-word for beginners.
Vocabulary list➡https://learning-korean.com/elementary/20210101-10466/

title: [ Elementary Korean 23 ]  ｰ겠어요 (intentions and plans)
description:
The theme of today’s class is 【내일은 날씨가 좋겠네요.】 “I guess the weather will be nice tomorrow.” 
Today, we will learn the grammar [ｰ겠어요] and I will summarize all five future tenses that we’ve learned so far. 

🔷Homework
1. Let's write Korean sentences

① Please use [verb+겠습니다] and write what you are determined to do.
Ex) 저는 매일 방을 청소하겠습니다.
        I will clean my room everyday.

② Please use [adjective+겠네요] “I guess “ and tell me about tomorrow’s weather in your area.
Ex) 내일은 하루 종일 비가 오겠네요.
       I guess it will be raining all day tomorrow.

2. Memorizing Vocabulary
40 words on the first page of my 1600-word for beginners.
Vocabulary list➡https://learning-korean.com/elementary/20210101-10466/

title: [ Elementary Korean 24 ]  Honorifics ,  (으)시다 ,  (으)세요
description:
The theme of today’s class is 【할머니께서는 잘 지내세요?】 “How is your grandmother?” 
In today’s class, we will learn honorifics such as [께, 께서는, 께서] ,and  [(으)시], [(으)세요] .

🔷Homework
1. Let's write Korean sentences

You can tell me about the hobbies and daily life of someone older than you, such as your parents, grandparents or your boss.
Ex1) 우리 아버님께서는 아침 다섯 시에 일어나세요.
         My father is waking up at five in the morning. 

Ex2) 그 후에 세수하시고 스트레칭하세요.
         After that, he washes his face and stretches his body.

2. Memorizing Vocabulary
40 words on the first page of my 1600-word for beginners.
Vocabulary list➡https://learning-korean.com/elementary/20210101-10466/

title: [ Elementary Korean 25 ]  Honorifics ,  -(이)세요 ,  -(으)십시오
description:
The theme of today’s class is 【어서 오십시오】 “Welcome to the store.”  
In today’s class, we will learn honorific sentence for nouns  [noun+(이)세요] “is/am/are”  and [(으)십시오] “Please do something”. 

Grammar Quiz (21-25 Lessons)👇
https://learning-korean.com/elementary/20210628-11094/

🔷Homework
1. Let's write Korean sentences

① Let's write [(이)세요] to write an occupation of a person who are older than you such as your boss, or your parents.
Ex) 우리 아버님께서는 요리사십니다. 
　   My father is a cook.

② Let's use [(으)십시오] to write a polite sentence. 
Ex) 밤에는 조용히 하십시오.
       Please, be quiet at night.

2. Memorizing Vocabulary
40 words on the first page of my 1600-word for beginners.
Vocabulary list➡https://learning-korean.com/elementary/20210101-10466/

title: [ Elementary Korean 26 ]  한테 / 한테서 , Past tense of honorifics
description:
The theme of today’s class is 【여기 계셨어요?】 “Were you here?” 
Today, we will learn three things. First, particles [한테/한테서] “to/from” Second, the past tense of honorifics for verbs or adjectives such as [(으)셨어요]. Third, the past tense of honorifics for nouns  [(이)셨어요].

🔷Homework
1. Let's write Korean sentences

① Let's use  [noun +(이)셨어요].
Ex) 우리 할머님께서는 간호사셨어요.
       My grandmother was a nurse.

② Let's use [(으)셨어요], past tense for honorifics.
Ex) 우리 할머님께서는 환자분들을 많이 아끼셨어요.
       My grandmother took very good care of her patients.

2. Memorizing Vocabulary
40 words on the first page of my 1600-word for beginners.
Vocabulary list➡https://learning-korean.com/elementary/20210101-10466/
Challenge the 40-Word Quiz➡https://learning-korean.com/elementary/20210705-11117/#toc12

title: [ Elementary Korean 27 ]  (으)로 ,  아/어 주세요 Can you do it (for me)?
description:
The theme of today’s class is 【펜을 빌려주시겠어요?】 “Can you lend me a pen?”
Today, you will learn [-(으)로] “to” and [아/어 주시겠어요? 아/어 주세요] “Can you do it (for me)?”

🔷Homework
1. Let's write Korean sentences

① Let's use [(으)로] to tell me about your plans for going out in the future.
Ex) 저는 다음 주말에 코스모스 공원으로 갈 거예요. 
       I am going to Cosmos Park next weekend.

② Let's use [아/어 주세요] to ask for a favor of someone politely. 
Ex) 사장님, 보너스 좀 올려 주세요.  
       Boss, please give me more bonus..

2. Memorizing Vocabulary
40 words on the first page of my 1600-word for beginners.

title: [ Elementary Korean 28 ]  아/어 주다/주시다/드리다 ,  아/어 보다/보이다
description:
The theme of today’s class is【가방이 비싸 보이네요】“The bag looks expensive”.
Today, we will learn 아/어 주다, “to do something for someone”. 
And 아/어 보다, “to try to do an action” and 아/어 보이다 “to look (adj).”

🔷Homework
1. Let's write Korean sentences

① Let's use 아/어 주다 “to do something for someone”.
Ex) 어머니가 저한테 사과를 보내 주셨어요. 
       IMy mother sent me apples. 

② Write sentences using the grammar 아/어 보다 “to try to do something”.
Ex) 올해는 춤을 배워 보세요! 
       Why don’t you try to learn a dance this year! 

2. Memorizing Vocabulary
40 words on the first page of my 1600-word for beginners.
Vocabulary list➡https://learning-korean.com/elementary/20210101-10466/

title: [ Elementary Korean 29 ]   -아/어서,  -(으)ㄹ 수 있어요/없어요
description:
The theme of today’s class is【유학 가서 한국어를 공부했어요】“I went to study abroad and studied Korean”.
Today, we will learn  [-아/어서] “because/and,” which is a word that expresses a cause and a reason, and [-(으)ㄹ 수 있어요/없어요] “can/can’t”.

🔷Homework
1. Let's write Korean sentences

① Let's use [아/어서] “because/so that/and”.
Ex) 한국어를 공부해서 자막없이 드라마를 보고 싶어요.
       I want to study Korean so that I can understand K-dramas without subtitles.

② Let's use [할 수 있다/없다] “can/cannot”.
Ex) 저는 응원봉을 돌릴 수 있어요.
       I can spin a baton.

2. Memorizing Vocabulary
40 words on the first page of my 1600-word for beginners.

title: [ Elementary Korean 30 ]  (으)니까 ,  못
description:
The theme of today’s class is【아직 운전 못 해요?】“You still can’t drive?”. 
Today, we will learn  (으)니까 (because), and 못 “can’t”.
By learning (으)니까 “because,” you will be able to say “I did this because ~”

Grammar Quiz (26-30 Lessons)👇
https://learning-korean.com/elementary/20210803-11193/

🔷Homework
1. Let's write Korean sentences

Let's use (으)니까 “because/so” and 못 “cannot” in a sentence.
Ex) 나는 애기가 있으니까 밤에는 일 못 해요.
       I have a baby, so I can’t work at night.

title: [ Elementary Korean 31 ]  지만 ,  -고 싶어요/싶어해요
description:
The theme of today's lesson is【스웨터를 교환하고 싶어요】"I would like to exchange my sweater."
Today we will study a connector, 지만 "but/although/even though", -고 싶어요 "want to", and -고 싶어해요 "Someone wants to."

🔷Homework
1. Let's write Korean sentences

Let’s use 지만 “but” and -고 싶어요 “want to” in a sentence.
Ex) 주말에는 쉬고 싶었지만 결국 일했어요. 
       I wanted to rest on the weekend, but I ended up working.

title: [ Elementary Korean 32 ]  (으)면 ,  (으)면서 ,  (으)러 가다/오다
description:
The theme of today’s class is 【복권에 당첨되면 뭘 하고 싶어요?】 “What would you want to do if you win the lottery?”
Today, we’ll learn (으)면 “if/when, (으)면서 “while/as” and (으)러 가다/오다 “to go/come (in order) to do something”.

🔷Homework
1. Let's write Korean sentences

① Please use (으)면 and make a sentence to say “if I~”.
Ex) 복권에 당첨되면 세계 여행을 하고 싶어요.
       If I win the lottery, I want to travel around the world.

② Please try making a sentence using (으)러 가다/오다 “to go/come (in order) to do something.”
Ex) 연예인을 만나러 팬미팅에 가요. 
       I’m going to a fan meeting to meet a celebrity.

2. Memorizing Vocabulary
40 words on the first page of my 1600-word for beginners.
Vocabulary list➡https://learning-korean.com/elementary/20210101-10466/

title: [ Elementary Korean 33 ]  -아/어도 돼요? ,  -(ㅂ)습니다만
description:
The theme of today’s class is【제가 가도 돼요?】 “Is it okay for me to go?”
Today, we’re going to learn two expressions. 
First, -아/어도 돼요? May I / Is it okay” which is used to ask someone for permission. 
Second, -(ㅂ)습니다만 “but/ however" which is a preface expression that is used when asking someone a favor. 

🔷Homework
1. Let's write Korean sentences

① Please use 아/어도 돼요 to write a sentence on asking for a permission.
Ex) 영화를 봐도 돼요? 
       May I watch a movie?

② Please use -(ㅂ)습니다만, -아/어 주시겠어요? “but, can you~?” to ask someone a favor in a polite manner. 
Ex) 죄송합니다만 길을 좀 알려 주시겠어요? 
       I’m sorry, but can you please teach me a direction?

2. Memorizing Vocabulary
40 words on the first page of my 1600-word for beginners.
Vocabulary list➡https://learning-korean.com/elementary/20210101-10466/
Challenge the 40-Word Quiz➡https://learning-korean.com/elementary/20210823-11230/#toc11

title: [ Elementary Korean 34 ]  -(으)려고 하다 ,  -(으)려고요 ,  -아/어야 되다
description:
The theme of today’s class is 【집에서 좀 쉬려고요】 “I am going to rest at home.”
Today, we will learn the grammar-(으)려고 하다 “plan to/intend to”  and -(으)려고요 “plan to.”
As you can see, these two are very similar expressions to show one’s intentions. 
In addition, we will learn  -아/어야 되다, which mean “ must / have to.”

🔷Homework
1. Let's write Korean sentences

① Let's use (으)려고 해요 “plan to, intend to” to explain what you’re going to do tomorrow.
Ex) 내일은 친구한테 전화를 걸으려고 해요. 
       I’m going to call my friend tomorrow.

② Let's use 아/어야 돼요 to explain something that you have to do by tomorrow.
Ex) 내일은 꼭 장 보러 가야 돼요. 
       I must go buy groceries tomorrow.

2. Memorizing Vocabulary
40 words on the first page of my 1600-word for beginners.
Vocabulary list➡https://learning-korean.com/elementary/20210101-10466/

title: [ Elementary Korean 35 ]  -는 ,  -는 게 좋다
description:
The theme of today’s class is 【택시 타고 가는 게 좋겠어요】 “I think it’s better to take a taxi.”
Today, we will learn how to modify nouns with action verbs  -는 “that (I) do,” and how to politely express your opinion to others with the expression  -는 게 좋다 “It’s better to (do an action)”. 

Grammar Quiz (31-35 Lessons)👇
https://learning-korean.com/elementary/20210906-11279/

🔷Homework
1. Let's write Korean sentences

Let's use 하는 게 좋다 that we learned today to give advice to someone, such as “It’s better to do~”.
Ex) 감기에 걸리면 빨리 들어가서 쉬는 게 좋아요.
       If you catch a cold, it’s better go home early to take a rest.

2. Memorizing Vocabulary
40 words on the first page of my 1600-word for beginners.
Vocabulary list➡https://learning-korean.com/elementary/20210101-10466/

title: [ Elementary Korean 36 ]  -(으)ㄴ ,  -(으)ㄴ 적이 있다/없다 ,  -(으)ㄴ 후에/다음에
description:
Today, we will learn how to modify nouns by making past tense, like (으)ㄴ+noun “that (I) did.”
If you learn how to use it, you can also apply to other expressions like 한 적이 있다/없다 “have done / have not done," -한 후에, -한 다음에 “after doing (something)."

🔷Homework
1. Let's write Korean sentences

① Let's use (으)ㄴ 적이 있어요 “have done- / have not done-” and tell me about your special experience that other people rarely had.
Ex) 브라질에서 야생 개미핥기를 직접 본 적이 있어요. 
       I have seen a wild anteater in Brazil.

40 words on the first page of my 1600-word for beginners.

title: [ Elementary Korean 37 ]  -(으)ㄹ+noun ,  (으)ㄴ/는/(으)ㄹ 것 같다
description:
Today, let’s learn how to modify nouns with verbs in a future tense such as -(으)ㄹ+noun, “that (I) will.” 
And by today, we have finally finished learning all the noun modifiers for verbs of past, present, and future tenses. The grammar that allows you to use all three tenses is (으)ㄴ/는/(으)ㄹ 것 같다 “looks/sounds like/appears that." 

🔷Homework
1. Let's write Korean sentences

Let's use  (으)ㄹ 것 같다 to guess what your friend or family member will likely do in the future. 
Ex) 우리 오빠는 다음 달에 결혼할 것 같아요. 
       My brother seems to get married next month.

2. Memorizing Vocabulary
40 words on the first page of my 1600-word for beginners.
Vocabulary list➡https://learning-korean.com/elementary/20210101-10466/

title: [ Elementary Korean 38 ]  (으)ㄴ+noun ,  잖아요 ,  군요
description:
The theme of today’s conversation is【백화점은 비싸잖아요】“Things in department stores are expensive, aren’t they?”
Today, we will learn a noun modifier for adjectives and two sentence endings, 하잖아요 “as you know/ you know” and 하군요 “I see (that)/indeed.”

🔷Homework
1. Let's write Korean sentences

① Let's use adjectives to write about the ideal person you want to be. 
Ex) 저는 똑똑한 사람이 되고 싶어요. 
       I want to become a smart person.

② Let's use 잖아요 to say a comment to someone close to you. 
Ex) 엄마, 내가 냉장고에 김치를 넣었잖아요. 
       Oh mom, I put the Kimchi into the refrigerator.

2. Memorizing Vocabulary
40 words on the first page of my 1600-word for beginners.

title: [ Elementary Korean 39 ]  Noun Modifier -인 ,  -아/어 있다
description:
The theme of today’s class is【현관 앞에 나와 있어요.】 “I’m out in front of the entrance door.”
Today, we will learn a noun modifier 인, “who is” and -아/어 있다 “is -ing/-ed” which expresses a continuing state of a completed action.  

🔷Homework
1. Let's write Korean sentences

 Let's make a sentence using -인 “who is,” and 아/어 있다 “to remain -ed.”
Ex) 내 친구인 유진이는 지금 일본에 와 있어요. 
       Yoojin who is my friend is in Japan right now.

title: [ Elementary Korean 40 ]  -는데 ,  았/었으면 좋겠다
description:
The theme of today’s class is【하나 물어보고 싶은데요】“I’d like to ask you one question.”
Today, we’ll learn about the grammar -는데 “but,” and 았/었으면 좋겠다 “I wish I could.” 

Grammar Quiz (36 - 40 Lessons)👇
https://learning-korean.com/elementary/20211011-11566/

🔷Homework
1. Let's write Korean sentences

① Let's use 는데 “but/however.” 
Ex) 극장에 너무 가고 싶은데 멀어서 못 가요. 
       I really want to go to the theater, but I can’t go because it’s far.

② Let's use 았/었으면 좋겠다 “I wish I can” to write your wishes for the next year.
Ex) 필리핀에 놀러 갔으면 좋겠어요. 
       I wish I can travel to the Philippines.

title: [ Elementary Korean 41 ]  -게  ,   게 되다 ,  -는 편이다
description:
The theme of today’s lesson is 【예쁘게 차려입고 어디 가는 길이에요?】 “Where are you going dressed up so nicely?”
Today, I will explain three grammar rules.  First,  -게 “~ly” to make an adjective into an adverb. Second, 게 되다 “become/end up doing,” and lastly, -는 편이다 “I tend to do~.”

🔷Homework
1. Let's write Korean sentences

① Let's use verb+-게 되다 “become/happen to” and tell us about a recent change you had.
Ex) 저는 이사가게 되었어요. 
       I happened to move a house.

② Let's use 는 편이다 “tend to” to tell us about what you do as a habit or try to do as a habit.
Ex) 저는 물을 많이 마시는 편이에요. 
       I tend to drink a lot.

2. Memorizing Vocabulary
40 words on the first page of my 1600-word for beginners.
Vocabulary list➡https://learning-korean.com/elementary/20210101-10466/

title: [ Elementary Korean 42 ]  -지 말고 ,  -지 마세요 ,  -지 못하다
description:
The theme of today’s class is 【걱정하지 마세요】 “Don’t worry.”
Today, we will learn the triple “지.” They are  -지 말고 “don’t do (this), but do (this)”, -지 마세요, “Please, don’t (do this),” and -지 못하다 “cannot.” 

🔷Homework
1. Let's write Korean sentences

① Let's use 지 말고 “don’t do this, but do this” to tell your friends or family about the action you want to encourage them to take.
Ex) 일에만 너무 집중하지 말고, 운동도 꾸준히 하세요. 
       “Don’t focus only on your work too much, but do some exercises as well.”

② Let's use -지 못하다 “cannot”  to tell me about something that you couldn’t do in the past, but can now.
Ex) 결혼 전에는 집안일을 잘 못 했지만 지금은 그럭저럭 하는 편이에요. 
       “Before I got married, I couldn’t do housework very well, but now I’m doing it somehow.”

2. Memorizing Vocabulary
40 words on the first page of my 1600-word for beginners.
Vocabulary list➡https://learning-korean.com/elementary/20210101-10466/
Challenge the 40-Word Quiz➡https://learning-korean.com/elementary/20211025-11605/#toc13

title: [ Elementary Korean 43 ] -(으)ㄹ 때 ,  -았/었을 때 ,  -(으)ㅂ시다
description:
The theme of today’s class is 【어렸을 때가 생각이 나네요】 “It reminds me of the time when I was little.”
Today, we will learn -(으)ㄹ 때 “when~” and -았/었을 때 “when (I) was~.” 
Then, we will learn -(으)ㅂ시다 “Let’s (do something)” and get to know how to suggest things to others.

🔷Homework
1. Let's write Korean sentences

① Let's use -았/었을 때 “when I was~” and write about a wonderful memory that you have.
Ex) 설악산에 갔을 때, 그 아름다운 경치에 말을 잃었어요. 
       “When I went to Mt. Sulak, the beautiful scenery made me speechless.”

② Let's use (으)ㅂ시다 “let’s (do something),” to suggest your friend or family to do something exciting.
Ex) 수족관에 놀러 갑시다! 
       “Let’s go to the aquarium!”

2. Memorizing Vocabulary
40 words on the first page of my 1600-word for beginners.
Vocabulary list➡https://learning-korean.com/elementary/20210101-10466/
Challenge the 40-Word Quiz➡https://learning-korean.com/elementary/20211101-11609/#toc11

title: [ Elementary Korean 44 ]  -(으)ㄴ 지 ,  /어지다 ,  -거나
description:
The theme of today’s class is 【날씨가 꽤 따뜻해졌네요】 “It’s gotten pretty warm these days.”
Today, we will learn three grammar rules. 
First, -(으)ㄴ 지 +time +되었다 “It has been (length of time) since.” 
Second,  아/어지다 “to become/turn,” and third, -거나 “or.” 

🔷Homework
1. Let's write Korean sentences

① Please tell us how long it has been since you’ve done something.
Ex) 서울에 이사온 지 벌써 3년이나 됐네요! 
       “It’s been already 3 years since I moved to Seoul!” 

② Let's use [과 / 와] to tell me your favorite Korean food, dramas or pop songs
Ex) 과거에 비해서 마음이 넓어졌어요. 
       “I became more generous than in the past.”

2. Memorizing Vocabulary
40 words on the first page of my 1600-word for beginners.
Vocabulary list➡https://learning-korean.com/elementary/20210101-10466/
Challenge the 40-Word Quiz➡https://learning-korean.com/elementary/20211108-11850/#toc13

title: [ Elementary Korean 45 ]  -기 전에 ,  -기로 하다
description:
The theme of today’s class is 【부산에서 만나기로 했어요】 “We decided to meet in Busan.”
Today, we will learn two grammar rules; -기 전에 “before (doing something.)” and  -기로 하다 “decide to do~.” 

Grammar Quiz (41-45 Lessons)👇
https://learning-korean.com/elementary/20211115-11860/

🔷Homework
1. Let's write Korean sentences

① Let's use -기 전에 “before (doing an action)” to tell us about some good habits that you always do before you do something.
Ex) 저는 아침에 세수하기 전에 몸무게를 재요. 
       “I weigh my body before I wash my face in the morning.” 

② Please tell us what you are determined to do this year!
Ex) 책을 적어도 한 달에 한 권은 읽기로 했어요. 
       “I decided to read at least one book in a month.”

2. Memorizing Vocabulary
40 words on the first page of my 1600-word for beginners.
Vocabulary list➡https://learning-korean.com/elementary/20210101-10466/

title: [ Elementary Korean 46 ]  Noun + 때문에 ,  -기 때문에 ,  -이/가 되다
description:
The theme of today’s class is 【재능이 있기 때문에 응원해 주고 싶어요】 “I want to support him because he has talent.”
Today, we will learn Noun+때문에 “because of noun, ” 기 때문에 “because,” and 이/가 되다 “to become.”

🔷Homework
1. Let's write Korean sentences

① Let's use -기 때문에 “because,” and tell us some mistakes you made at work or school and the reason why you made the mistake. 
Ex) 유튜브를 보면서 요리를 만들었기 때문에 먹는 시간이 늦어졌어요. 
       “The dinner time became late because I cooked while watching YouTube.”

② Let's use 이/가 되다 and tell us what you want to be in next 10 years. 
Ex) 10년 후에는 세계로 통하는 한국어 강사가 되고 싶어요. 
       “10 years later, I want to become a world-class Korean teacher.”

2. Memorizing Vocabulary
40 words on the first page of my 1600-word for beginners.
Vocabulary list➡https://learning-korean.com/elementary/20210101-10466/

title: [ Elementary Korean 47 ]  -기 쉽다/어렵다 ,  -기 위해서
description:
The theme of today’s class is 【직접 가서 사기도 해요】 “I sometimes go and buy them in person.”
Today, we will learn the grammar -기 쉽다/어렵다 “it is easy to /difficult to” and 기 위해서 “in order to.” 

🔷Homework
1. Let's write Korean sentences

① Let's use -기 쉽다/어렵다 “easy/difficult to.” 
Ex) 이 춤은 따라하기 쉬워요. 
       “This dance is easy to imitate.”

② Let's use -기 위해서 “in order to” and tell us what you all are working hard for.
Ex) 회사를 잘 운영하기 위해서 여러가지 책을 읽고 있어요. 
       “I am reading various kinds of books in order to run my company well.”

2. Memorizing Vocabulary
40 words on the first page of my 1600-word for beginners.
Vocabulary list➡https://learning-korean.com/elementary/20210101-10466/

title: [ Elementary Korean 48 ]  -(이)라고 하다 ,  -냐고 하다
description:
The theme of today’s class is 【같이 못 오냐고 해요】”He’s asking if you can come together with us.”
Today I’ll teach you about indirect quotations such as -(이)라고 하다 ”(someone) says that” and -냐고 하다”asks/suggests.” 

🔷Homework
1. Let's write Korean sentences

① Let's  use -(이)래요 to tell me about something that somebody told you.
Ex) 호떡이 지금 한국에서 가장 인기가 많은 음식이래요. 
       “I heard Hoeddeok is currently the most popular food in Korea.” 

② Let's use -냬요 to tell me about a question that a loved one or friend has asked you. 
Ex) 친구가 오늘 시간이 있냬요. 
       “My friend is asking if you have time today.”

2. Memorizing Vocabulary
40 words on the first page of my 1600-word for beginners.
Vocabulary list➡https://learning-korean.com/elementary/20210101-10466/
Challenge the 40-Word Quiz➡https://learning-korean.com/elementary/20211206-11933/#toc11

title: [ Elementary Korean 49 ]  -는다고 하다 (-는대요)
description:
Theme of today’s lesson is 【몇 시에 온대요?】”What time did he say he would come?”
Today is the second lesson for an indirect quotation! Whoolay! 
We will learn 는다고 하다 “It is said that/I heard +(someone) does” and 는대요, which is the shortened version of 는다고 해요.  

🔷Homework
1. Let's write Korean sentences

① Please make a sentence using -ㄴ/는다고 해요 “I heard/it is said.”
Ex) 오늘은 우리 엄마가 요리를 한다고 해요. 
       “I heard that my mother is cooking today."

② Let’s use 는대요, a shortened version of -ㄴ/는다고 해요 “I heard/it is said.”
Ex) 내일은 날씨가 좋대요. 
       “I heard that it will be good weather tomorrow.” 

2. Memorizing Vocabulary
40 words on the first page of my 1600-word for beginners.
Vocabulary list➡https://learning-korean.com/elementary/20210101-10466/
Challenge the 40-Word Quiz➡https://learning-korean.com/elementary/20211213-11937/#toc9

title: [ Elementary Korean 50 ] -(으)라고 하다 , -자고 하다
description:
The theme of today’s class is 【우리가 정하래요】 “He told us to decide.”
Today, we will learn the indirect quotation -(으)라고 하다 “tell (someone) to do (something)," and indirect quotation for suggestions -자고 하다 “suggest (someone) do." 

Grammar Quiz (46-50 Lessons)👇
https://learning-korean.com/elementary/20211220-11970/

🔷Homework
1. Let's write Korean sentences

① Let's use -(으)라고 하다 or -(으)래요 “tell (someone) to do (something).”
Ex) 우리 엄마가 빨리 빨래하라고 했어요. 
       “My mother told me to do the laundry quickly.” 

② Let's use [과 / 와] to tell me your favorite Korean food, dramas or pop songs
Ex) 절친이 같이 애완견 박람회에 가자고 했어요.  
       “My best friend suggested that we go to a pet expo together.”

2. Memorizing Vocabulary
40 words on the first page of my 1600-word for beginners.
Vocabulary list➡https://learning-korean.com/elementary/20210101-10466/
Challenge the 40-Word Quiz➡https://learning-korean.com/elementary/20211220-11965/#toc13

・The test is 40 minutes long.

title: [Emma's seemile Korean language class 07] Make Korean sentences, 존이 식당에 가요, 저는 차를 안 마셔요, 카이는 생선을 사요?
description:
A new Korean language class, taught by Emma, has begun!
Emma has a Korean teaching license. And she has experience teaching Korean to many foreigners. She teaches Korean very kindly. So, she will be a good guide for those who are learning Korean for the first time. If you listen to this class, you may understand why we call Emma kind.

★★★ If you have any questions about Korean after watching the lesson, please leave a comment. We'll do our best to answer that question. ★★★

▣ This lesson contains: 
▶ Present tenses
• V-아요/어요(Declarative) : 존이 식당에 가요.
• 안 V-아요/어요 (Negative) : 존이 서점에 안 가요.
• V-지 않아요 (Negative) : 존이 서점에 가지 않아요.
• V-아요/어요? (Interrogative) : 존은 식당에 안 가요?
▶ Postpositional particle
• N이/가 : 존이 의자에 않아요.

title: [Emma's seemile Korean language class 08] Present tenses A-아요/어요, 안 A-아요/어요, A-지 않아요, A-아요/어요?
description:
A new Korean language class, taught by Emma, has begun!
Emma has a Korean teaching license. And she has experience teaching Korean to many foreigners. She teaches Korean very kindly. So, she will be a good guide for those who are learning Korean for the first time. If you listen to this class, you may understand why we call Emma kind.
We hope you enjoy this class and that your Korean language skills will grow rapidly.

★★★ If you have any questions about Korean after watching the lesson, please leave a comment. We'll do our best to answer that question. ★★★

▣ This lesson contains: Present tenses
• A-아요/어요 (Declarative) : 저는 머리가 길어요.
• 안 A-아요/어요 (Negative) : 저는 머리가 안 짧아요.
• A-지 않아요 (Negative) : 저는 머리가 짧지 않아요.
• A-아요/어요? (Interrogative) : 아람 씨는 머리가 길어요?

▣ Let's check the words.
'키가 크다' is 'tall' in English, and '키가 작다' is 'short' in English.
However, if you translate the words '크다' and '작다', which are often used in Korean, into English, they are 'big or large' and 'small'.
• 우리 집은 크다 (My house is big.)
• 저기에 큰 탁자가 있다 (There is a large table over there.) 
• 우리 집은 작다. (My house is small.)
• 저기에 작은 탁자가 있다 (There is a small table over there.)

▣ seemile Korean 
• Book  https://www.seemile.com/product/seemile-korean/

title: [Emma's seemile Korean language class 37] 만약, 만일, 그리고, 그래서, 그런데, 그러나, 또, 하지만
description:
A new Korean language class, taught by Emma, has begun!
Emma has a Korean teaching license. And she has experience teaching Korean to many foreigners. She teaches Korean very kindly. So, she will be a good guide for those who are learning Korean for the first time. If you listen to this class, you may understand why we call Emma kind.
We hope you enjoy this class and that your Korean language skills will grow rapidly.

★★★ If you have any questions about Korean after watching the lesson, please leave a comment. We'll do our best to answer that question. ★★★

▣ This lesson contains: 
• 만약
  만약 카이가 노래한다면 존은 귀를 막을 거예요.
• 만일
  만일 히토미가 졸업한다면 한국에서 취직할 거예요.
• 그리고
  카이는 주문을 받았어요. 그리고 요리하기 시작했어요.
• 그래서
  히토미는 졸업했어요. 그래서 한국 회사에 취직했어요.
• 그런데
  카이는 주문을 받았어요. 그런데 요리할 수 없었어요.
• 그러나
  존은 컴퓨터를 켰어요. 그러나 기사를 입력하지는 않았어요.
• 또
  카이는 생선을 샀어요. 또 채소도 샀어요.
• 하지만
  히토미는 일찍 출발했어요. 하지만 수업에 늦었어요.

title: [Day1] Learn 10 Korean Words per Day - Korean Vocabulary Playlist
description:
This is my new contents "10 Korean Words per DAY"! Goal is to learn and memorize 300 basic Korean vocabulary per month , that is, 10 Words per day X 30 days ! [Day 1]

Play it randomly during your day - in the metro, bus, on your way to school, to work - and naturally memorize 10 words per day without sparing extra time.

title: Learn 50 Essential Korean Words in One 5-Minute Story - Slow Korean for Beginners
description:
Hello Korean learners! Get the full lesson and transcriptions for this video here:
https://pandatwin.com/korean-ms/-Xi9TnR4dU8

When you click the link above, you'll find:
📜 The Full Transcript (follow along in Korean and English)
🧠 Interactive Quiz (test how much you understood)
📖 Vocabulary List (key words and phrases from the video)
...and many more resources to help you improve!

---
📜 ***The Full Script (Read Along With Us!)*** 📜
---
민준: 서연아, 뭐 해?
서연: 민준아, 그냥 앉아 있어. 심심하다.
민준: 나도 심심해. 정말 정말 심심해.
서연: 우리 뭐 재미있는 거 할까?
민준: 좋아! 뭐 할까?
서연: 음... 아! 좋은 생각 있어! 우리 게임 하자.
민준: 게임? 무슨 게임이야?
서연: 기억력 게임! 아주 재미있어.
민준: 기억력 게임? 어떻게 하는 거야?
서연: 아주 쉬워. 내가 알려줄게. 자, 여기 테이블을 봐.
민준: 응. 테이블이 있어.
서연: 이제 테이블 위에 물건을 놓자. 여기 책이 있어.
민준: 좋아. 나는 펜이 있어. 테이블 위에 펜.
서연: 좋아. 그리고 여기 사과. 테이블 위에 사과.
민준: 여기 컵도 있어. 테이블 위에 컵.
서연: 아주 좋아. 자, 이제 테이블 위에 뭐가 있어?
민준: 음... 책, 펜, 사과, 컵이 있어.
서연: 맞아. 잘 봐. 이제... 눈 감아.
민준: 알겠어. 눈 감았어.
서연: (서연이가 펜을 숨긴다) 자, 이제 눈 떠.
민준: 응. 눈 떴어. 어?
서연: 테이블을 잘 봐. 지금 뭐가 없어?
민준: 음... 잠깐만. 책은 있어.
서연: 응, 책은 있어.
민준: 그리고 사과도 있어. 컵도 있어.
서연: 맞아. 그럼 뭐가 없지?
민준: 아! 알겠다! 펜! 펜이 없어!
서연: 와! 맞았어! 민준이 대단하다!
민준: 하하. 이거 정말 재미있다.
서연: 그렇지? 재미있지?
민준: 응! 이제 내 차례야. 서연이, 준비됐어?
서연: 응! 준비됐어.
민준: 자, 테이블 위에 책, 펜, 사과, 컵. 다 있어. 이제 눈 감아.
서연: 알겠어. 눈 감았어. 보지 않을게.
민준: (민준이가 사과를 숨긴다) 서연아, 이제 눈 떠!
서연: 응, 떴어. 어디 보자... 음...
민준: 하하. 알겠어?
서연: 잠깐만... 펜은 있어.
민준: 응, 펜은 있어.
서연: 책도 있고... 컵도 있어.
민준: 맞아. 그럼 뭐가 없어?
서연: 아! 사과! 빨간 사과가 없어!
민준: 정답! 맞았어! 서연이도 정말 잘한다!
서연: 와! 이 게임 정말 재미있네.
민준: 응. 이제 하나도 안 심심해.
서연: 우리 한 번 더 하자!
민준: 좋아! 이번에도 내가 할게. 눈 감아!

---
What do you think of this topic? Let us know in the comments! 👇 Don't forget to like and subscribe for more bite-sized Korean lessons! ❤️

channel_title: Learn Korean with Min-jun and Seo-yeon
title: Korean Beginner Lesson: Must-Know Phrases for Gifts 🎁
description:
Hello Korean learners! Get the full lesson and transcriptions for this video here:
https://pandatwin.com/korean-ms/JQFKGieuPoQ

When you click the link above, you'll find:
📜 The Full Transcript (follow along in Korean and English)
🧠 Interactive Quiz (test how much you understood)
📖 Vocabulary List (key words and phrases from the video)
...and many more resources to help you improve!

---
📜 ***The Full Script (Read Along With Us!)*** 📜
---
민준: 서연아, 어서 와.
서연: 안녕, 민준. 이게 뭐야?
민준: 이거 할머니 선물이야.
서연: 와, 선물! 정말 좋다. 상자가 크네.
민준: 응. 그런데... 포장을 잘 못 해. 서연아, 도와줄 수 있어?
서연: 물론이지. 포장, 재미있어. 우리 같이 하자.
민준: 정말? 고마워! 그럼 뭐가 필요해?
서연: 음... 포장지, 가위, 그리고 테이프가 필요해.
민준: 알겠어. 여기 포장지 있어. 이 포장지 어때?
서연: 와, 파란색 포장지네. 정말 예쁘다.
민준: 나도 이 포장지가 좋아. 자, 이제 시작하자.
서연: 좋아. 먼저, 상자를 포장지 위에 놓자.
민준: 알겠어. 이렇게? 상자를 가운데에 놓으면 돼?
서연: 응, 맞아. 아주 잘했어. 이제 가위가 필요해.
민준: 여기 가위. 내가 자를게.
서연: 좋아. 조심해서 천천히 잘라.
민준: 응. 천천히... 자, 다 잘랐어.
서연: 정말 잘 잘랐다. 이제 포장지를 접자.
민준: 이렇게 접는 거 맞아? 조금 어렵다.
서연: 괜찮아. 내가 도와줄게. 이쪽을 먼저 접고... 그리고 이쪽을 접어.
민준: 아하! 알겠어. 이제 테이프가 필요해.
서연: 맞아. 테이프. 테이프는 어디에 있어?
민준: 여기 있어. 내가 테이프를 줄게.
서연: 고마워. 자, 여기를 딱 붙이자.
민준: 와! 정말 재미있다. 여기도 테이프를 붙일까?
서연: 응, 좋아. 거기도 붙여. 튼튼하게.
민준: 됐다! 와, 거의 다 했어. 정말 예쁘다.
서연: 응. 우리가 같이 하니까 더 예쁜 것 같아.
민준: 이제 뭐 하면 돼?
서연: 음... 마지막으로 리본! 리본을 달면 더 예쁠 거야.
민준: 아, 리본! 좋은 생각이야. 여기 빨간색 리본이 있어. 어때?
서연: 빨간색 리본? 파란색 포장지랑 잘 어울려. 정말 예쁘겠다.
민준: 그럼 내가 리본을 붙일게. 이렇게... 됐다!
서연: 짜잔! 완성! 정말 멋진 선물 포장이야.
민준: 와! 우리가 했어. 정말 마음에 들어. 할머니가 정말 좋아하시겠지?
서연: 그럼! 당연하지. 정말 좋아하실 거야.
민준: 서연아, 정말 고마워. 너 없었으면 못 했을 거야.
서연: 아니야. 같이 하니까 정말 재미있었어. 다음에 또 같이 하자.

---
What do you think of this fun gift-wrapping adventure? Let us know in the comments! 👇 Don't forget to like and subscribe for more bite-sized Korean lessons! ❤️

channel_title: Learn Korean with Min-jun and Seo-yeon
title: Learn Korean with a Story: Aquarium Essential Vocabulary! 🐠 (Beginner Level). Korean Lesson for You
description:
Hello Korean learners! Get the full lesson and transcriptions for this video here:
https://pandatwin.com/korean-ms/2m1Vk-1YcNo

When you click the link above, you'll find:
📜 The Full Transcript (follow along in Korean and English)
🧠 Interactive Quiz (test how much you understood)
📖 Vocabulary List (key words and phrases from the video)
...and many more resources to help you improve!

---
📜 ***The Full Script (Read Along With Us!)*** 📜
---
민준: 서연, 여기 아쿠아리움이에요. (Minjun: Seoyeon, this is the aquarium.)
서연: 와! 정말 커요. 그리고 정말 좋아요. (Seoyeon: Wow! It's really big. And really nice.)
민준: 네, 맞아요. 우리 들어가요. (Minjun: Yes, that's right. Let's go in.)
서연: 좋아요! 빨리 물고기 보고 싶어요. (Seoyeon: Great! I want to see the fish quickly.)
민준: 좋아요. 같이 들어가요. (Minjun: Okay. Let's go in together.)
서연: 와! 민준, 저기 좀 봐요. (Seoyeon: Wow! Minjun, look over there.)
민준: 우와! 물고기예요. (Minjun: Ooh! It's a fish.)
서연: 네, 물고기예요. 물고기가 정말 많아요. (Seoyeon: Yes, it's a fish. There are so many fish.)
민준: 정말 많아요. 그리고 정말 예뻐요. (Minjun: There are so many. And they're really pretty.)
서연: 맞아요. 정말 예뻐요. (Seoyeon: That's right. They're really pretty.)
민준: 이 물고기는 파란색이에요. (Minjun: This fish is blue.)
서연: 저 물고기는 노란색이에요. (Seoyeon: That fish is yellow.)
민준: 파란색 물고기도 있고, 노란색 물고기도 있어요. (Minjun: There are blue fish and yellow fish.)
서연: 이 물고기들은 작아요. (Seoyeon: These fish are small.)
민준: 네, 아주 작아요. 정말 귀여워요. (Minjun: Yes, they're very small. Really cute.)
서연: 여기 다른 물고기도 있어요. 이쪽으로 와요. (Seoyeon: There are other fish here too. Come this way.)
민준: 와! 서연, 이 물고기는 정말 커요. (Minjun: Wow! Seoyeon, this fish is really big.)
서연: 네, 정말 커요. 아주 아주 커요. (Seoyeon: Yes, it's really big. Very, very big.)
민준: 저쪽 물고기들은 작아요. (Minjun: Those fish over there are small.)
서연: 맞아요. 저쪽 물고기들은 작아요. 그런데 이 물고기는 정말 커요. (Seoyeon: That's right. Those fish over there are small. But this fish is really big.)
민준: 정말 신기해요. (Minjun: It's really amazing/fascinating.)
서연: 네, 저도 정말 신기해요. (Seoyeon: Yes, it's really amazing to me too.)
민준: 서연, 저것은 뭐예요? 물고기 아니에요. (Minjun: Seoyeon, what is that? It's not a fish.)
서연: 음… 저게 뭐예요? (Seoyeon: Hmm… what is that?)
민준: 아! 저것은 해파리예요. (Minjun: Ah! That's a jellyfish.)
서연: 해파리요? 와, 정말 예뻐요. (Seoyeon: A jellyfish? Wow, it's really pretty.)
민준: 네, 해파리가 정말 예뻐요. 색깔이 정말 예뻐요. (Minjun: Yes, the jellyfish is really pretty. The colors are really pretty.)
서연: 맞아요. 정말 신기하게 생겼어요. (Seoyeon: That's right. It looks really fascinating/unique.)
민준: 여기 해파리가 많아요. (Minjun: There are many jellyfish here.)
서연: 네, 작은 해파리도 있어요. 큰 해파리도 있어요. (Seoyeon: Yes, there are small jellyfish. There are big jellyfish too.)
민준: 서연! 저기 거북이도 있어요. (Minjun: Seoyeon! There's a turtle over there too.)
서연: 어디요? 아! 정말 거북이가 있어요. (Seoyeon: Where? Ah! There really is a turtle.)
민준: 거북이가 수영해요. (Minjun: The turtle is swimming.)
서연: 와, 정말 귀여워요. (Seoyeon: Wow, it's really cute.)
민준: 거북이가 천천히 수영해요. 아주 천천히요. (Minjun: The turtle is swimming slowly. Very slowly.)
서연: 맞아요. 정말 천천히 움직여요. (Seoyeon: That's right. It moves really slowly.)
민준: 거북이, 안녕? (Minjun: Hello, turtle?)
서연: 하하. 민준, 거북이는 대답이 없어요. (Seoyeon: Haha. Minjun, the turtle doesn't answer.)
민준: 그렇네요. 그래도 정말 귀여워요. (Minjun: You're right. But it's still really cute.)
서연: 민준, 오늘 정말 재미있어요. (Seoyeon: Minjun, today is really fun.)
민준: 나도 정말 재미있어요. (Minjun: It's really fun for me too.)
서연: 아쿠아리움은 처음이에요. 정말 좋아요. (Seoyeon: This is my first time at an aquarium. It's really good.)
민준: 저도 처음이에요. 물고기도 보고, 해파리도 보고, 거북이도 봤어요. (Minjun: It's my first time too. I saw fish, jellyfish, and turtles.)
서연: 정말 즐거웠어요. (Seoyeon: It was really enjoyable.)
민준: 다음에 또 와요. (Minjun: Let's come again next time.)
서연: 네, 좋아요! 다음에 꼭 또 와요. (Seoyeon: Yes, great! Let's definitely come again next time.)

---

channel_title: Learn Korean with Min-jun and Seo-yeon
title: Learn Korean with a Slow Easy Story: Room/Furniture Essential Vocabulary (Korean Beginner Lesson)
description:
Hello Korean learners! Get the full lesson and transcriptions for this video here:
https://pandatwin.com/korean-ms/z-kiAnGhvCI

When you click the link above, you'll find:
📜 The Full Transcript (follow along in Korean and English)
🧠 Interactive Quiz (test how much you understood)
📖 Vocabulary List (key words and phrases from the video)
...and many more resources to help you improve!
---
📜 ***The Full Script (Read Along With Us!)*** 📜
---
민준: 서연, 어서 와. 여기는 내 방이야.
서연: 안녕, 민준. 와, 여기가 민준이 방이구나.
민준: 응. 들어와. 편하게 앉아.
서연: 고마워. 와, 방이 정말 좋다.
민준: 정말? 고마워, 서연.
서연: 민준. 이거 뭐예요?
민준: 아, 이거? 이것은 내 책상이야.
서연: 와, 책상이네. 책상이 아주 커요.
민준: 응. 나는 큰 책상이 좋아.
서연: 정말 크고 좋다.
민준: 고마워.
서연: 그럼, 이거는 뭐예요? 책상 앞에 있어.
민준: 그것은 내 의자야.
서연: 의자구나. 의자도 아주 좋아 보여.
민준: 응. 이 의자 아주 편해.
서연: 책상 위에 컴퓨터도 있네.
민준: 응. 맞아. 내 컴퓨터야.
서연: 컴퓨터, 정말 좋다.
민준: 고마워.
서연: 민준, 저기 저거는 뭐예요?
민준: 저거? 저것은 내 침대야.
서연: 와, 침대. 침대가 정말 넓고 깨끗하다.
민준: 하하. 오늘 아침에 청소했어.
서연: 그렇구나. 침대가 정말 좋아 보여.
민준: 고마워, 서연.
서연: 그럼 침대 옆에 저거는 뭐예요?
민준: 아, 저것은 책장이야.
서연: 책장이구나. 와, 책이 정말 많다.
민준: 응. 책이 아주 많아.
서연: 민준은 책을 많이 읽는구나.
민준: 응. 나는 책 읽는 것을 좋아해.
서연: 와, 멋지다. 민준, 방이 정말 좋아.
민준: 정말? 고마워.
서연: 응. 아주 깨끗하고, 아주 밝아.
민준: 기분 좋다. 이제 우리 여기 앉아서 이야기하자.
서연: 응, 좋아!

---
